articles.title: D'oh! I can't believe I did that! Graceful ways to handle awkward moments
articles.summary: Yes, there is a way to gracefully address that you've been walking around with spinach in your teeth. Here are tactics to quickly defuse your most embarrassing, cringe-inducing moments.
articles.titleZh: 噢！我不敢相信我竟然这么做了！处理尴尬时刻的优雅方法
articles.summaryZh: 是的，有一种方法可以优雅地解决您一直叼着菠菜走来走去的问题。这里有一些技巧可以帮助你快速化解最尴尬、最令人畏缩的时刻。

contentParagraphs.en: marabird/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 马拉鸟/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: On my first day of a new job about 15 years ago, I walked right into the glass door of a conference room in front of all my new colleagues.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大约 15 年前，在我开始新工作的第一天，我当着所有新同事的面走进了会议室的玻璃门。

contentParagraphs.en: Did I play it cool? No. I did the most awkward thing imaginable. I yelled, "OUCH!" — interrupting a presentation and causing everyone to rush over and fuss over me. I wanted to hide under the table until the meeting was over.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我玩得很酷吗？不，我做了你能想象到的最尴尬的事。我大喊：“哎哟！” ——打断了演示，导致大家都冲过来对我大惊小怪。我想躲在桌子底下直到会议结束。

contentParagraphs.en: Whenever I experience something embarrassing, I somehow make the situation worse. Being quick on my feet has never been my strong suit.
contentParagraphs.zh: 每当我经历一些尴尬的事情时，我就会以某种方式让情况变得更糟。脚步敏捷从来都不是我的强项。

contentParagraphs.en: There are, however, a few go-to tactics you can deploy to handle cringe-inducing moments with ease and grace, say experts. As I've learned from this story, I should have just quickly assured everyone I was OK, apologized for disrupting and sat down without giving it another thought.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，专家表示，您可以采取一些首选策略来轻松优雅地处理令人畏缩的时刻。正如我从这个故事中学到的那样，我应该迅速向每个人保证我没事，为打扰而道歉，然后坐下来，不再多想。

contentParagraphs.en: Uncomfortable things are bound to happen in life, and that's OK. The key is to know how to manage them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 生活中难免会发生一些不舒服的事情，这没关系。关键是要知道如何管理它们。

contentParagraphs.en: "The goal is not to eliminate awkwardness from your life," said Ali Mattu, a clinical psychologist, in a Life Kit episode about social anxiety. "The goal is to navigate awkwardness, because every connection you want — the friends you want to make, the work opportunities you want to gain — are all on the other side."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们的目标不是消除生活中的尴尬，”临床心理学家阿里·马图 (Ali Mattu) 在有关社交焦虑的《生活工具包》一集中说道。 “我们的目标是克服尴尬，因为你想要的每一个联系——你想要结交的朋友，你想要获得的工作机会——都在另一边。”

contentParagraphs.en: Here are five real-life awkward situations you might find yourself in, and how to respond in the moment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以下是您在现实生活中可能会遇到的五种尴尬情况，以及当下如何应对。

contentParagraphs.en: The situation: You've been talking with spinach in your teeth
contentParagraphs.zh: 情况：你一直用牙齿叼着菠菜说话

contentParagraphs.en: The fix: Acknowledge it and move on
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决办法：承认并继续

contentParagraphs.en: Reported by Andee Tagle
contentParagraphs.zh: 安迪·塔格莱报道

contentParagraphs.en: If you run face-first into an embarrassing situation, just face the thing head-on. Put it all right out on the table.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果你遇到尴尬的局面，就正面面对吧。把一切都摆在桌子上。

contentParagraphs.en: You might say, "Oh, wow, I am so sorry you've had to look at that spinach between my teeth. That was awkward," says Ty Tashiro, a psychologist and a social scientist.
contentParagraphs.zh: 你可能会说，“哦，哇，我很抱歉你不得不看我牙齿间的菠菜。这太尴尬了，”心理学家兼社会科学家泰·田代说。

contentParagraphs.en: "It shows the other person that you understand what the social expectation is and that you don't intend to continue being awkward in that way," he says. "And it allows you to move on from the moment."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这向对方表明你了解社会期望是什么，并且你不打算继续以这种方式感到尴尬，”他说。 “它可以让你从现在开始继续前进。”

contentParagraphs.en: If you don't put the awkwardness on the table, he says, "it has this weird way of lingering through the rest of the conversation." For tips on how to stop cringing about an awkward moment, read our comic.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说，如果你不把尴尬摆在桌面上，“它就会以一种奇怪的方式在接下来的谈话中挥之不去。”有关如何停止因尴尬时刻而畏缩的提示，请阅读我们的漫画。

contentParagraphs.en: The situation: You hate compliments, and someone just gave you one
contentParagraphs.zh: 情况：你讨厌赞美，而有人刚刚给了你赞美

contentParagraphs.en: The fix: Just say thank you
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决办法：只说谢谢

contentParagraphs.en: Reported by Andee Tagle
contentParagraphs.zh: 安迪·塔格莱报道

contentParagraphs.en: Many of us are bad at taking compliments. We deflect, negate, freeze up and sometimes visibly cringe. When there's a disconnect between how we see ourselves and how others see us, that can be uncomfortable, says Erica Boothby, a social psychologist.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我们中的许多人都不善于接受赞美。我们会偏转、否定、冻结，有时还会明显感到畏缩。社会心理学家艾丽卡·布斯比 (Erica Boothby) 表示，当我们看待自己的方式与他人看待我们的方式不一致时，可能会感到不舒服。

contentParagraphs.en: Compliments can be especially challenging for people who struggle with self-esteem, says Xuan Zhao, a behavioral scientist. They may feel like they don't deserve the praise.
contentParagraphs.zh: 行为科学家赵轩表示，对于那些在自尊心上挣扎的人来说，赞美尤其具有挑战性。他们可能觉得自己不值得赞扬。

contentParagraphs.en: Even if it feels hard, there are good reasons to accept a compliment. One study has found that genuine praise makes you feel happier. "When you receive a compliment, the reward circuit of your brain lights up, as if you were receiving money," Zhao says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 即使感觉很困难，也有充分的理由接受赞美。一项研究发现，真诚的赞美会让你感觉更快乐。 “当你收到赞美时，你大脑的奖励回路就会亮起来，就像你收到了钱一样，”赵说。

contentParagraphs.en: But there's no need to linger. Acknowledge the comment with a simple thank-you and move on, Zhou says. For more insights on the science of compliments, read our comic.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但没必要耽搁。周说，用简单的感谢来确认评论，然后继续前进。有关赞美科学的更多见解，请阅读我们的漫画。

contentParagraphs.en: The situation: People love to comment on your appearance, and it makes you feel like a spectacle
contentParagraphs.zh: 情况：人们喜欢评论你的外表，这让你感觉自己很引人注目

contentParagraphs.en: The fix: Make a joke of it, but if it keeps happening, call it out
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决办法：开个玩笑，但如果这种情况持续发生，就大声喊出来

contentParagraphs.en: Reported by Andee Tagle
contentParagraphs.zh: 安迪·塔格莱报道

contentParagraphs.en: You can give someone one pass with a little joke to let them know you don't like it, says Brittany Luse, host of It's Been a Minute. "You can respond with, 'Gosh, you're the first person to tell me that.'" It gives them the opportunity to laugh with you, but it also lets them know that you're not playing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《It's Been a Minute》的主持人布列塔尼·卢斯 (Brittany Luse) 表示，你可以给某人讲一个小笑话，让他们知道你不喜欢这个笑话。 “你可以这样回应，‘天哪，你是第一个告诉我这个的人。’”这让他们有机会和你一起笑，但也让他们知道你不是在玩。

contentParagraphs.en: If people continue to cross this boundary, speak up, says Adia Gooden, a clinical psychologist and host of the podcast Unconditionally Worthy. "You can say, 'It's not cool. I'm not comfortable with it. Please stop.'"
contentParagraphs.zh: 临床心理学家、播客《Unconditionally Worthy》的主持人阿迪亚·古登 (Adia Gooden) 表示，如果人们继续跨越这一界限，请大声说出来。 “你可以说，‘这不酷。我对此感到不舒服。请停下来。’”

contentParagraphs.en: "You're not saying they're an awful person, but you're communicating that what they said is problematic," she says. Read the full response to this Dear Life Kit question here.
contentParagraphs.zh: “你并不是说他们是一个糟糕的人，但你是在传达他们说的话是有问题的，”她说。请在此处阅读对“亲爱的生活工具包”问题的完整答复。

contentParagraphs.en: The situation: Your aunt puts you on the spot at a family dinner
contentParagraphs.zh: 情况：你的阿姨在家庭聚餐时让你难堪

contentParagraphs.en: The fix: Be direct … or redirect
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决办法：直接……或重定向

contentParagraphs.en: Reported by Marielle Segarra
contentParagraphs.zh: 玛丽埃尔·塞加拉报道

contentParagraphs.en: If your aunt keeps asking when you're getting married (a topic you don't want to get into), it's fine to say, "I don't want to talk about it," says Nedra Glover Tawwab, a therapist who specializes in relationships and the author of Set Boundaries, Find Peace.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果你的阿姨一直问你什么时候结婚（这是一个你不想讨论的话题），你可以说“我不想谈论这件事”，内德拉·格洛弗·塔瓦布（Nedra Glover Tawwab）说，他是一位专门研究人际关系的治疗师，也是《设定界限，寻找和平》一书的作者。

contentParagraphs.en: You could also give a response like, "My thoughts about it aren't fully processed. I'm still in the thinking phase about it."
contentParagraphs.zh: 你也可以这样回答：“我对此的想法尚未完全处理。我仍处于思考阶段。”

contentParagraphs.en: If the moment feels right, you can use humor to redirect the conversation. You might say, "Whoa! That's a big question," says Tawwab. "It can be a way to switch the topic without being so heavy." For more advice on how to manage awkward conversations at family parties, click here.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果时机合适，你可以用幽默来引导谈话。您可能会说，“哇！这是一个大问题，”塔瓦布说。 “这可能是一种转换话题而又不那么沉重的方式。”有关如何处理家庭聚会中尴尬对话的更多建议，请单击此处。

contentParagraphs.en: The situation: You messed up while speaking in public
contentParagraphs.zh: 情况：你在公共场合演讲时搞砸了

contentParagraphs.en: The fix: Take a beat
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决方法：稍稍休息一下

contentParagraphs.en: Reported by Kyle Norris
contentParagraphs.zh: 凯尔·诺里斯报道

contentParagraphs.en: If something unexpected happens in the moment — say you stumble or lose your place — pause or slow down.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果此刻发生了意外的事情——比如你绊倒了或者失去了自己的位置——暂停或放慢速度。

contentParagraphs.en: "If you get tongue-tied, take a beat," says Eva Margarita, former assistant director of Texas Speech, the speech team at the University of Texas at Austin. Take a deep breath, and go back to the beginning of a sentence or restate the word you stumbled on.
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果你张口结舌，那就停下来，”德克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校演讲团队德克萨斯演讲的前助理主任伊娃·玛格丽塔说。深吸一口气，然后回到句子的开头或重述您偶然发现的单词。

contentParagraphs.en: And don't worry — people don't notice those pauses as much as you think they do, says Lauren Dominguez Chan, former speechwriter for former first lady Jill Biden and former U.S. Surgeon General Vivek Murthy. "Even if someone does notice, that's OK too." Get more confidence-boosting tips on public speaking here.
contentParagraphs.zh: 前第一夫人吉尔·拜登和前美国卫生局局长维韦克·穆尔蒂的前演讲撰稿人劳伦·多明格斯·陈表示，别担心，人们并没有像你想象的那样注意到这些停顿。 “即使有人注意到了，那也没关系。”在这里获取更多关于公开演讲的增强信心的技巧。

contentParagraphs.en: This story was edited by Meghan Keane. The visual editor is CJ Riculan. We'd love to hear from you. Leave us a voicemail at 202-216-9823, or email us at LifeKit@npr.org.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这个故事由梅根·基恩编辑。视觉编辑是 CJ Riculan。我们很乐意听取您的意见。请致电 202-216-9823 给我们留言，或发送电子邮件至 LifeKit@npr.org。

contentParagraphs.en: Listen to Life Kit on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, and sign up for our newsletter. Follow us on Instagram: @nprlifekit.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在 Apple 播客和 Spotify 上收听 Life Kit，并订阅我们的时事通讯。在 Instagram 上关注我们：@nprlifekit。

articles.title: China's Moonshot AI claims Kimi K3 can rival OpenAI and Anthropic
articles.summary: The company has unveiled a massive new artificial intelligence model it says can take on top American firms.
articles.titleZh: 中国的Moonshot AI声称Kimi K3可以与OpenAI和Anthropic相媲美
articles.summaryZh: 该公司推出了一个大规模的新人工智能模型，据称可以与美国顶尖公司抗衡。

contentParagraphs.en: Chinese AI start-up Moonshot has unveiled a massive new artificial intelligence model it says can rival top American firms.The company launched Kimi K3, containing 2.8 trillion parameters, which serves as a measure of an AI's scale and processing power. Kimi K3's full capabilities – coding, knowledge work, and reasoning – will be known when it is released as an open-source model on 27 July. The sudden breakthrough suggests that China's tech prowess is rapidly narrowing the capabilities gap, upending long-held assumptions in the West that Chinese developers trail their American peers. Its arrival later this month will make it the world's first open-source model in the three-trillion-parameter class that can be freely downloaded, run and customised by outside developers. The release comes at a highly sensitive moment for the global technology sector, just weeks after the US government abruptly forced American developer Anthropic to temporarily withdraw its flagship Fable and Mythos models due to severe cybersecurity concerns. While Washington has since lifted those restrictions, the initial move highlights how the US government now views advanced AI software as critical national infrastructure, labelling frontier models as vital national security assets subject to strict export controls. However, the rapid arrival of Kimi K3 suggests Chinese firms are successfully bypassing these regulatory barriers and advancing independently despite US restrictions on hardware sales. Heavily backed by domestic tech giants Alibaba and Tencent, Moonshot has quickly risen to the forefront of China's generative AI ecosystem. In a statement the company said that K3 stands as Moonshot AI's "most capable flagship model to date". Unlike closed, proprietary American systems from OpenAI or Anthropic, Kimi K3's open nature allows global users to modify the system for advanced reasoning and complex software development. Moonshot AI noted that the system is uniquely built to operate with "minimal human supervision" to sustain tasks such as engineering and coding. Third-party evaluations from Artificial Analysis and Arena.ai show the model performing on a par with leading models in the US, such as OpenAI's GPT and Anthropic's Claude. In these independent benchmarks, external, Kimi K3 ranked first in web interface engineering, outperforming Anthropic's Fable system, external in blind human-preference tests. While the system's massive size means running it locally requires significant computing equipment, making it open-source could heavily disrupt Silicon Valley's commercial models. The announcement had an immediate impact on shares in Moonshot's domestic competitors Zhipu and MiniMax, which tumbled sharply in Hong Kong by about 27% and 16% respectively.
contentParagraphs.zh: 中国人工智能初创公司Moonshot推出了一个大规模的新人工智能模型，据称可以与美国顶级公司相媲美。该公司推出了Kimi K3，包含2.8万亿个参数，可作为人工智能规模和处理能力的衡量标准。 Kimi K3 的全部功能——编码、知识工作和推理——将于 7 月 27 日作为开源模型发布时为人所知。这一突然的突破表明，中国的科技实力正在迅速缩小能力差距，颠覆了西方长期以来认为中国开发商落后于美国同行的假设。它将于本月晚些时候推出，这将使其成为世界上第一个拥有 3 万亿参数类别的开源模型，可以由外部开发人员免费下载、运行和定制。此次发布正值全球科技行业高度敏感的时刻，就在几周前，由于严重的网络安全问题，美国政府突然迫使美国开发商 Anthropic 暂时撤回其旗舰 Fable 和 Mythos 模型。虽然华盛顿后来取消了这些限制，但最初的举动凸显了美国政府现在如何将先进的人工智能软件视为关键的国家基础设施，将前沿模型标记为重要的国家安全资产，并受到严格的出口管制。然而，Kimi K3的迅速到来表明，中国企业正在成功绕过这些监管障碍，在美国限制硬件销售的情况下独立发展。在国内科技巨头阿里巴巴和腾讯的大力支持下，Moonshot 迅速跻身中国生成人工智能生态系统的前列。该公司在一份声明中表示，K3 是 Moonshot AI “迄今为止最强大的旗舰机型”。与 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 的封闭式专有美国系统不同，Kimi K3 的开放性允许全球用户修改系统以进行高级推理和复杂的软件开发。 Moonshot AI 指出，该系统的构建独特，可以在“最少的人工监督”下运行，以维持工程和编码等任务。 Artificial Analysis 和 Arena.ai 的第三方评估显示，该模型的性能与 OpenAI 的 GPT 和 Anthropic 的 Claude 等美国领先模型相当。在这些独立的外部基准测试中，Kimi K3 在网页界面工程方面排名第一，在盲人偏好测试中优于 Anthropic 的 Fable 系统。虽然该系统规模庞大，意味着在本地运行需要大量计算设备，但将其开源可能会严重扰乱硅谷的商业模式。该公告立即对 Moonshot 的国内竞争对手智普和 MiniMax 的股价产生了影响，它们在香港分别大幅下跌约 27% 和 16%。

articles.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
articles.summary: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.
articles.titleZh: 中国暗示美国可能恢复香港贸易特权
articles.summaryZh: 这一决定是在唐纳德·特朗普总统与习近平会面两个月后作出的，这可能会在习近平预计访美之前使两国关系升温。

contentParagraphs.en: FILE - The U.S. and Chinese flag at the Great Hall of the People prior to the state dinner of President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping on May 14, 2026, in Beijing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 文件 - 2026 年 5 月 14 日在北京举行的唐纳德·特朗普总统和中国国家主席习近平国宴之前，人民大会堂悬挂的美国和中国国旗。

contentParagraphs.en: HONG KONG — China signaled on Friday that the United States could restore Hong Kong 's preferential privileges, saying Washington confirmed it will not renew an executive order that revoked the city's special trading status.
contentParagraphs.zh: 香港——中国周五表示，美国可以恢复香港的优惠待遇，称华盛顿确认不会延长撤销香港特殊贸易地位的行政命令。

contentParagraphs.en: The Commerce Ministry said that the U.S. made commitments on Hong Kong issues and other matters during the U.S.-China trade talks in Madrid last year. The U.S. recently confirmed to China that the President's Executive Order on Hong Kong Normalization would end, the ministry said in a statement responding to media questions.
contentParagraphs.zh: 商务部表示，去年马德里中美贸易谈判期间，美方就香港问题等作出了承诺。外交部在回应媒体提问的声明中表示，美国最近向中方确认，总统关于香港正常化的行政命令将终止。

contentParagraphs.en: "The U.S. side's actions represent an important step in fulfilling the consensus reached during the bilateral economic and trade talks. China appreciates it," it said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美方此举是落实两国经贸磋商共识的重要一步，中方对此表示赞赏。

contentParagraphs.en: It is not immediately clear what all the implications of the decision are. The White House referred questions about the executive order lapsing to the Treasury Department.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前尚不清楚该决定的所有影响是什么。白宫将有关行政命令失效的问题转交给财政部。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control said in a statement Friday that the national emergency declared in the executive order had expired and that it delisted people who were sanctioned under the order. But it said people who remain sanctioned under another act related to Hong Kong have been added to a different sanction list.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国外国资产控制办公室周五在一份声明中表示，行政命令中宣布的国家紧急状态已经到期，并已将受该命令制裁的人员除名。但它表示，根据另一项与香港有关的法案仍然受到制裁的人已被添加到另一个制裁名单中。

contentParagraphs.en: The statement showed Hong Kong leader John Lee and his predecessor, Carrie Lam, were removed from the first list but added to the second one.
contentParagraphs.zh: 声明显示，香港领导人李约翰和他的前任林郑月娥已从第一个名单中删除，但被添加到第二个名单中。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. decision came two months after President Donald Trump met with his counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing. It could warm ties between them ahead of Xi's expected visit to the U.S. later this year. Earlier this month, a pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October was released after Trump brought up his case with Xi.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在北京会见习近平两个月后做出了这一决定。在习近平预计今年晚些时候访问美国之前，这可能会暖化两国之间的关系。本月早些时候，一名于 10 月份在中国被拘留的著名地下教会牧师在特朗普向习近平提出案件后获释。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump signed the now-expired executive order in July 2020, during his first term in response to Beijing imposing a national security law that year. Trump's order was last renewed for a year in July 2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普于 2020 年 7 月在其第一个任期内签署了现已过期的行政命令，以回应北京当年实施的国家安全法。特朗普的命令上次延长一年是在 2025 年 7 月。

contentParagraphs.en: Under the order, Trump said Hong Kong was no longer sufficiently autonomous to justify differential treatment in relation to mainland China under certain laws. It eliminated the preferential treatment for Hong Kong to the extent permitted by law and in the national security, foreign policy, and economic interest of the United States.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据该命令，特朗普表示，香港不再具有足够的自治权，无法证明根据某些法律对中国大陆实行差别待遇是合理的。它在法律允许的范围内以及符合美国国家安全、外交政策和经济利益的情况下取消了对香港的优惠待遇。

contentParagraphs.en: China considers the national security law for Hong Kong necessary to restore stability in the city after massive anti-government protests in 2019. The pro-democracy movement back then posed one of the biggest challenges to the Communist Party in Beijing and the Hong Kong government since the former British colony returned to Chinese rule in 1997.
contentParagraphs.zh: 中国认为，在2019年大规模反政府抗议活动之后，香港的国家安全法对于恢复香港的稳定是必要的。自1997年这个前英国殖民地回归中国统治以来，当时的民主运动对北京共产党和香港政府构成了最大的挑战之一。

contentParagraphs.en: Six years after the law's introduction, many leading activists, including pro-democracy former media tycoon Jimmy Lai, were imprisoned under it. Critics say the Western-style civil liberties that Beijing promised to maintain for 50 years after the handover have declined.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该法律出台六年后，许多主要活动人士，包括民主派前媒体大亨黎智英，都根据该法律被监禁。批评者称，北京承诺在回归后维持50年的西式公民自由已经下降。

contentParagraphs.en: Hong Kong government said in a statement that it noted the "positive shift in the U.S. policy" toward the city.
contentParagraphs.zh: 香港政府在一份声明中表示，它注意到美国对香港的“政策的积极转变”。

contentParagraphs.en: "Safeguarding Hong Kong's prosperity and stability serves the common interests of China and the US, and also aligns with the general expectation of the international community," it said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 维护香港繁荣稳定符合中美共同利益，也符合国际社会普遍期待。

contentParagraphs.en: It said it hopes the U.S. will respect China's sovereignty and the rule of law in Hong Kong and resume normal economic and trade exchanges with the city.
contentParagraphs.zh: 希望美方尊重中国主权和香港法治，与香港恢复正常经贸往来。

articles.title: Diamonds and 'cab to orbit': Rocket aiming to transform India's space ambitions takes off
articles.summary: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.
articles.titleZh: 钻石和“驾驶室送入轨道”：旨在改变印度太空雄心的火箭起飞
articles.summaryZh: 印度首个航天科技独角兽 Skyroot Aerospace 于周六成功发射 Vikram-1。

contentParagraphs.en: What if launching a satellite was as easy as calling a cab? That's the vision behind an Indian private rocket company that achieved its first orbital launch on Saturday.Skyroot Aerospace, which recently became India's first space tech unicorn after reaching $1.1bn valuation, launched Vikram-1 from the Indian Space Research Organisation's (Isro) launch facility in Sriharikota in southern India.The seven-storey rocket lifted off at 12:05 India time [06:35 GMT] and travelled 280 miles (450km) to reach the Low Earth Orbit in a 16-minute flight.Skyroot is the first Indian private company to launch a rocket into orbit, making India only the third country, after the US and China, with a private company capable of orbital launches."Vikram-1 Test Flight-1 has reached orbit... History is made," the company posted , externalon X.The successful Vikram-1 launch will take Skyroot closer to its goal of offering what it calls a "cab service to space", where companies can hire a rocket "to ride to a unique location in the orbit to place a satellite or visit a space station".The rocket - named after Vikram Sarabhai, who is called the father of India's space programme - is small and has the capacity to carry payloads of up to 350kg, Skyroot co-founder and CEO Pawan Kumar Chandana told the BBC before the launch.Chandana says today, access to space remains "a major bottleneck, with satellite operators often waiting for months or even years for a launch opportunity" and that their venture expects to change that.He says Skyroot aims to cut long waits for satellite launches by offering dedicated missions for small payloads. Instead of sharing space on large rockets that fly on fixed schedules, customers can book a launch tailored to their satellite and its required orbit - much like taking a taxi instead of waiting for a train."If you want to just go to a friend's house, you don't need a train, you book a cab, an Uber. What we are offering is a cab service to space, which can be used to ride to a unique location in the orbit to place a satellite or visit a station."Skyroot's model would appear similar to that of Rocket Lab in the US, which provides small-lift launch vehicles. The Indian test launch mission called Aagman - Sanskrit for arrival - has placed into orbit six payloads. They include scientific instruments such as a robotic arm for removing space debris, an Earth observation camera and satellites, including one from a German company. But they also include two symbolic payloads which have generated a buzz in India. One is a lotus made of lab-grown diamonds and a tiny gold rocket with micro-sculptures of three of India's best-known scientists.Each smaller than a grain of rice, the sculptures pays tribute to Nobel Prize-winning physicist CV Raman and aerospace engineer and former Indian president APJ Abdul Kalam, besides Sarabhai. "We exist because of the Indian space programme, we stand on the shoulders of our early visionaries and this is our way of paying tribute to three great scientists who shaped India's space programme," Chandana explained.He said the diamond lotus - called Cosmic Bloom and developed by Cosmos Diamonds - is an artist's tribute to space and celebrates India's creativity. It is expected to remind us all of the line "like a diamond in the sky" - from the popular nursery rhyme Twinkle, Twinkle.Saturday's launch is the first of two test flights Skyroot plans to do this year before they launch commercially next year."We have the capacity to build one rocket every month at our factory in the southern city of Hyderabad," Chandana said. Skyroot came into being in 2018 when Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka - who were colleagues at Isro - quit their jobs and co-founded the space-tech start-up to build rocket components for satellites.In 2020, India opened the space sector to private firms, allowing them to build rockets and satellites and use Isro's launch facilities with the aim of increasing the country's share in the global business market from 2% at present to 10% by 2030. Since then, the Indian government says more than 400 space start-ups have been set up in India, but Skyroot remains the most successful of them - and the only unicorn in the sector. The company first made headlines in November 2022 when it launched India's first privately developed suborbital rocket.Saturday's launch came at a time when India's space programme has been in focus following Isro's historic Moon, Mars and solar missions in recent years. India plans to send astronauts into space next year, an orbiter to Venus by 2028 and build its own space station by 2035. And Skyroot's cab service could also cater to Isro's space programmes, but Chandana says that "70-80% of our market would be the global economy"."These would include satellites supporting services that millions rely on every day, from agriculture and fisheries to disaster management, communications, connectivity, navigation and national security. So, the economic opportunity is huge."
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果发射卫星就像叫出租车一样简单怎么办？这就是一家印度私人火箭公司在周六实现首次轨道发射的愿景。Skyroot Aerospace 公司在估值达到 11 亿美元后，最近成为印度第一家太空科技独角兽公司，从印度空间研究组织 (Isro) 位于印度南部 Sriharikota 的发射设施发射了 Vikram-1。这枚七层火箭于印度时间 12:05 [格林威治标准时间 06:35] 升空，飞行 280 英里（450 公里）到达在 16 分钟的飞行中到达近地轨道。Skyroot 是第一家将火箭发射到轨道的印度私营公司，使印度成为继美国和中国之后第三个拥有能够进行轨道发射的私营公司的国家。该公司在 X 网站上发布消息称：“Vikram-1 Test Flight-1 已经进入轨道……历史已经创造。”Vikram-1 的成功发射将使 Skyroot 更接近其提供所谓的“太空出租车服务”的目标，公司可以在该服务中租用火箭Skyroot 联合创始人兼首席执行官帕万·库马尔·钱达纳 (Pawan Kumar Chandana) 在发射前向 BBC 表示，这枚火箭以被称为印度太空计划之父的维克拉姆·萨拉巴伊 (Vikram Sarabhai) 命名，体积较小，能够运载高达 350 公斤的有效载荷。钱达纳表示，今天，进入太空仍然是“一个主要瓶颈，卫星运营商常常等待数月甚至数年的发射机会”，并且他们的合资企业希望改变这种状况。他说，Skyroot 的目标是通过为小型有效载荷提供专用任务来缩短卫星发射的漫长等待时间。客户可以预订根据自己的卫星及其所需轨道量身定制的发射服务，而不是在固定时间表飞行的大型火箭上共享空间，就像乘坐出租车而不是等火车一样。“如果你只想去朋友家，你不需要火车，你可以预订一辆出租车或优步。我们提供的是到太空的出租车服务，可以用来乘坐到轨道上的一个独特位置来放置卫星或访问空间站。”Skyroot 的模型看起来与此类似。美国火箭实验室，提供小型运载火箭。印度的测试发射任务名为“Aagman”（梵语，意为“到达”），已将六个有效载荷送入轨道。其中包括科学仪器，例如用于清除空间碎片的机械臂、地球观测相机和卫星，其中包括一家德国公司的卫星。但它们还包括两个在印度引起轰动的象征性有效载荷。其中一个是一朵由实验室培育的钻石制成的莲花，还有一个微型黄金火箭，上面有三位印度最著名科学家的微型雕塑。这些雕塑每个都比一粒米还小，除了萨拉巴伊之外，还向诺贝尔奖获得者物理学家CV拉曼、航空航天工程师、印度前总统APJ阿卜杜勒卡拉姆致敬。钱达纳解释说：“我们的存在是因为印度太空计划，我们站在早期梦想家的肩膀上，这是我们向塑造印度太空计划的三位伟大科学家致敬的方式。”他说，钻石莲花——名为“宇宙之花”，由 Cosmos Diamonds 开发——是艺术家对太空的致敬，也是对印度创造力的颂扬。预计它会让我们所有人想起流行童谣《一闪一闪》中的“像天空中的钻石”这句台词。周六的发射是 Skyroot 计划今年进行的两次试飞中的第一次，然后明年进行商业发射。“我们位于南部城市海得拉巴的工厂有能力每月制造一枚火箭，”钱达纳说。 Skyroot 成立于 2018 年，当时 Isro 的同事 Chandana 和 Naga Bharath Daka 辞去了工作，共同创立了这家航天科技初创公司，为卫星制造火箭部件。2020 年，印度向私营企业开放航天领域，允许它们制造火箭和卫星，并使用 Isro 的发射设施，目标是到 2020 年将印度在全球商业市场的份额从目前的 2% 提高到 10%。 2030 年。印度政府表示，自那时以来，印度已经成立了 400 多家太空初创企业，但 Skyroot 仍然是其中最成功的，也是该领域唯一的独角兽。该公司于 2022 年 11 月首次成为头条新闻，当时它发射了印度第一枚私人开发的亚轨道火箭。周六的发射正值印度空间计划近年来成为印度航天计划历史性的月球、火星和太阳任务之后的焦点之际。印度计划明年将宇航员送入太空，在 2028 年将轨道飞行器送入金星，并在 2035 年建造自己的空间站。Skyroot 的出租车服务也可以满足印度空间研究组织的太空计划，但钱达纳表示，“我们的市场 70-80% 将是全球经济”。经济机会是巨大的。”

articles.title: Kalshi says it's not a sportsbook even as World Cup bets surge
articles.summary: The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.
articles.titleZh: 卡尔希表示，即使世界杯投注激增，这也不是体育博彩
articles.summaryZh: 投注网站 Kalshi 在世界杯期间成为主要的体育投注平台。但该公司坚称自己不是体育博彩运营商，从而避免了数十亿美元的税收。

contentParagraphs.en: FILE - An ad for the prediction market app Kalshi is displayed on a mobile phone on April 16, 2026, in Chicago. (AP Photo/Erin Hooley, File)
contentParagraphs.zh: 文件 - 2026 年 4 月 16 日，芝加哥，手机上显示预测市场应用 Kalshi 的广告。 （美联社照片/Erin Hooley，档案）

contentParagraphs.en: The World Cup has been a sports betting bonanza for Kalshi.
contentParagraphs.zh: 世界杯对卡尔希来说是一笔体育博彩的财富。

contentParagraphs.en: Since the soccer tournament started, the company has set new trading records, taking in around $40 billion in sports wagers, according to the prediction market analytics company Ticker Tracker, which uses Kalshi's public data.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据使用 Kalshi 公开数据的预测市场分析公司 Ticker Tracker 的数据，自足球锦标赛开始以来，该公司创下了新的交易记录，体育投注收入约为 400 亿美元。

contentParagraphs.en: Compare that to the $4 billion analysts are expecting online sportsbooks like DraftKings and FanDuel to log in bets across all 104 matches.
contentParagraphs.zh: 相比之下，分析师预计 DraftKings 和 FanDuel 等在线体育博彩公司将在所有 104 场比赛中记录下注金额为 40 亿美元。

contentParagraphs.en: The disparity is fueling skepticism from sportsbook advocates over Kalshi's position that it is not a sports gambling app.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这种差异加剧了体育博彩倡导者对 Kalshi 的立场的怀疑，即它不是体育赌博应用程序。

contentParagraphs.en: To the sports betting industry, Kalshi and other prediction market operators have found a legal backdoor to grow sports betting without paying state gaming taxes. Additionally, Kalshi's app has effectively lowered the sports betting age from 21 to 18 and entered states where betting on sports is illegal, like California and Texas, thanks to the Trump administration's endorsement of Kalshi's argument that it is something other than a sports gambling site.
contentParagraphs.zh: 对于体育博彩行业来说，卡尔希和其他预测市场运营商已经找到了一个合法的后门，可以在不缴纳州博彩税的情况下发展体育博彩。此外，Kalshi 的应用程序有效地将体育博彩年龄从 21 岁降低到 18 岁，并进入了体育博彩非法的州，如加利福尼亚州和德克萨斯州，这要归功于特朗普政府对 Kalshi 的论点的认可，即它不是体育博彩网站。

contentParagraphs.en: Kalshi says, technically, it is a federally regulated financial product, not a sports betting site. On Kalshi, bettors wager against each other, rather than betting against "the house," like with sportsbooks. The company does not make money when bettors lose. Instead, it tacks a fee onto every transaction. Kalshi also points to all the other things it allows people to bet on, like elections, the president's word choice and the week's top-streaming movie on Netflix.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡尔希说，从技术上讲，它是一种受联邦监管的金融产品，而不是体育博彩网站。在卡尔希，投注者相互下注，而不是像体育博彩那样对“庄家”下注。当投注者输钱时，公司不会赚钱。相反，它会对每笔交易收取费用。卡尔希还指出了它允许人们押注的所有其他事情，例如选举、总统的用词选择以及 Netflix 本周流媒体最多的电影。

contentParagraphs.en: Kalshi spokeswoman Elisabeth Diana adds that, unlike sportsbooks, Kalshi does not restrict bets or ban people who are consistently big winners.
contentParagraphs.zh: Kalshi 发言人 Elisabeth Diana 补充说，与体育博彩不同，Kalshi 不会限制投注或禁止那些一直是大赢家的人。

contentParagraphs.en: "Just like the stock market, you can exit your position at any time. It's a fairer, less predatory platform," Diana said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “就像股票市场一样，你可以随时退出头寸。这是一个更公平、掠夺性更少的平台，”戴安娜说。

contentParagraphs.en: Whether Kalshi is an unlicensed sportsbook or a totally new type of finance app may seem like a pedantic distinction, but the question is at the center of more than 20 pending federal lawsuits, with billions of dollars of tax revenue and the future of the fast-growing prediction market industry at stake.
contentParagraphs.zh: Kalshi 是一款未经许可的体育博彩公司还是一款全新类型的金融应用程序，这似乎是一种迂腐的区分，但这个问题是 20 多起悬而未决的联邦诉讼的核心，涉及数十亿美元的税收收入和快速增长的预测市场行业的未来。

contentParagraphs.en: Australia's Aiden O'Neill (13) battles for the ball with Paraguay's Matias Galarza (23) during the World Cup Group.
contentParagraphs.zh: 澳大利亚队的艾登·奥尼尔（13 岁）在世界杯小组赛中与巴拉圭队的马蒂亚斯·加拉尔萨（23 岁）争夺球权。

contentParagraphs.en: "From a bettor's standpoint, Kalshi and sportsbooks are basically identical," said Victor Matheson, an economics professor at the College of the Holy Cross who specializes in sports gambling. "I don't see why they should be regulated and taxed any differently than sportsbooks."
contentParagraphs.zh: 圣十字学院专门研究体育博彩的经济学教授维克多·马西森 (Victor Matheson) 表示：“从博彩玩家的角度来看，卡尔希和体育博彩基本上是相同的。” “我不明白为什么他们的监管和税收应该与体育博彩有任何不同。”

contentParagraphs.en: Kalshi may be trying to distance itself from sports betting apps, but it is also trying to lure people away from them.
contentParagraphs.zh: Kalshi 可能试图与体育博彩应用保持距离，但它也试图吸引人们远离它们。

contentParagraphs.en: A recent digital ad campaign across YouTube, Facebook and Snapchat featured a sunglasses-clad monkey wearing soccer jerseys in speeding cars and lounging in bars with disco balls with the caption: "Life after switching from predatory sportsbooks to using Kalshi."
contentParagraphs.zh: 最近 YouTube、Facebook 和 Snapchat 上的数字广告活动展示了一只戴着墨镜、穿着足球衫的猴子在飞驰的汽车中，懒洋洋地躺在放着迪斯科球的酒吧里，标题是：“从掠夺性体育博彩转向使用 Kalshi 后的生活。”

contentParagraphs.en: After NPR asked Kalshi about the ads, they appear to have been removed from each company's searchable library of digital advertisements. Kalshi says the ads are still running.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在 NPR 询问 Kalshi 有关这些广告的问题后，这些广告似乎已从每家公司的可搜索数字广告库中删除。卡尔希说广告仍在投放。

contentParagraphs.en: Is Kalshi a 'sportsbook with a small prediction market business attached?'
contentParagraphs.zh: Kalshi 是一家“附带小型预测市场业务的体育博彩公司吗？”

contentParagraphs.en: Kalshi does not see itself as a sports betting app, but sports are, by far, the most popular type of wagering on Kalshi, typically accounting for about 80 to 90% of all bets placed on the site.
contentParagraphs.zh: Kalshi 并不认为自己是一款体育博彩应用程序，但到目前为止，体育是 Kalshi 上最受欢迎的投注类型，通常占该网站所有投注的 80% 至 90% 左右。

contentParagraphs.en: One popular type of betting is when traders put money down on what team will win or lose and on "parlays," which Kalshi calls a "combo," or bets on multiple things happening in one game. Then there are wagers on points scored in the first half of the game, an individual player's performance, what sports announcers will say live and endless other possible outcomes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一种流行的投注类型是，交易者将资金押在哪支球队将获胜或输掉以及“连本带利”（Kalshi 称之为“组合”）上，即对一场比赛中发生的多项事情进行投注。然后还有对上半场比赛得分、个别球员的表现、体育播音员现场直播的内容以及无数其他可能结果的投注。

contentParagraphs.en: "Kalshi is a sportsbook with a small prediction market business attached to it," said David Forman, the vice president of research at the American Gaming Association, an industry group representing casinos and betting sites.
contentParagraphs.zh: “Kalshi 是一家体育博彩公司，附属于小型预测市场业务，”代表赌场和博彩网站的行业组织美国博彩协会 (American Gaming Association) 的研究副总裁戴维·福尔曼 (David Forman) 表示。

contentParagraphs.en: DraftKings is one of the leading mobile sports betting sites, which view Kalshi as an unlicensed rival.
contentParagraphs.zh: DraftKings 是领先的移动体育博彩网站之一，该网站将 Kalshi 视为未经许可的竞争对手。

contentParagraphs.en: "When people are betting on sports they should realize it's an entertainment product and they are ultimately going to lose money, but they should have a good time doing it," Forman said. "Whereas these products are out there pitching themselves as financial investments, which is irresponsible."
contentParagraphs.zh: 福尔曼说：“当人们在体育博彩上投注时，他们应该意识到这是一种娱乐产品，他们最终会赔钱，但他们应该玩得很开心。” “而这些产品却将自己标榜为金融投资，这是不负责任的。”

contentParagraphs.en: The association's data tracking shows the past 10 months have seen the biggest slowdown in legal sports betting since it became widely available in the U.S. nearly a decade ago, according to Forman. During the same time period, prediction market apps like Kalshi and Polymarket have been on a growth tear. Last year, Kalshi traders' monthly betting totalled around $5 billion. Last month, more than $30 billion was bet on the platform.
contentParagraphs.zh: 福曼表示，该协会的数据跟踪显示，过去 10 个月合法体育博彩出现了自近十年前在美国广泛普及以来的最大放缓。同一时期，Kalshi 和 Polymarket 等预测市场应用程序一直在快速增长。去年，Kalshi 交易员的每月投注总额约为 50 亿美元。上个月，该平台上的赌注超过 300 亿美元。

contentParagraphs.en: So far though, the prediction market insurgents have not meaningfully cut into sportsbook profits. That's because the slump has been in the amount of money bet, known in gambling industry lingo as "the handle," which is not where sportsbooks turn a profit. Sports betting sites make money by taking a portion of bettors' losses. DraftKings and FanDuel have both reported upticks in quarterly profits in recent months, as bettors increasingly turn to smaller-dollar bets and the companies cut back cash bonuses, according to Brad Allen, a gambling industry analyst at Eilers & Krejcik Gaming.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但到目前为止，预测市场的颠覆者还没有大幅削减体育博彩的利润。这是因为投注金额的下滑，在赌博行业行话中被称为“手柄”，这不是体育博彩盈利的地方。体育博彩网站通过承担投注者的部分损失来赚钱。 Eilers & Krejcik Gaming 博彩业分析师布拉德·艾伦 (Brad Allen) 表示，随着投注者越来越多地转向小额投注，而且两家公司削减了现金奖金，DraftKings 和 FanDuel 的季度利润近几个月均有所上升。

contentParagraphs.en: "Kalshi and Polymarket are signing up customers in states where sports betting isn't allowed, like California and Texas, and signing up 18-year-olds in states where the legal age to sports bet is 21," Allen said, "It's broadly new money, money sports
contentParagraphs.zh: 艾伦说：“Kalshi 和 Polymarket 在不允许体育博彩的州（例如加利福尼亚州和德克萨斯州）签约客户，并在体育博彩法定年龄为 21 岁的州签约 18 岁的青少年。”

articles.title: The new $1 Trump coin doesn't just buck norms. Experts say it also breaks laws
articles.summary: President Trump's face will appear on a new commemorative coin honoring the nation's 250th birthday. It's one of the many unusual places his likeness has popped up this year.
articles.titleZh: 新的 1 美元特朗普硬币不仅违背了规范。专家称这也违反法律
articles.summaryZh: 特朗普总统的头像将出现在纪念美国 250 岁生日的新纪念币上。这是今年他的肖像出现的众多不寻常的地方之一。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. Mint is producing a new $1 coin featuring President Trump's image. He's only the second living president to get this honor, after Calvin Coolidge in 1926.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国造币厂正在生产一枚印有特朗普总统头像的新 1 美元硬币。他是继 1926 年卡尔文·柯立芝之后第二位获此殊荣的在世总统。

contentParagraphs.en: In his second term, President Trump's image has cropped up in many new places: U.S. passports, national park passes, government building banners and — soon — official coins.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在特朗普总统的第二个任期内，他的形象出现在许多新的地方：美国护照、国家公园通行证、政府大楼的横幅以及即将出现的官方硬币。

contentParagraphs.en: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent unveiled designs this week for a commemorative $1 gold-colored coin, which he said the U.S. Mint will start producing in honor of the country's 250th birthday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 财政部长斯科特·贝森特 (Scott Bessent) 本周公布了 1 美元金色纪念币的设计，他表示美国造币厂将开始生产该纪念币，以纪念美国建国 250 周年。

contentParagraphs.en: One side features the bald eagle from the distinctive U.S. seal. The other shows a close-up of Trump — wearing a suit, tie and serious expression — encircled by the word "Liberty" and "1776-2026."
contentParagraphs.zh: 一侧饰有独特的美国印章中的秃鹰。另一幅是特朗普的特写镜头——穿着西装，打着领带，表情严肃——周围环绕着“自由”和“1776-2026”一词。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's very cute, they gave me a coin," Trump told Fox News on Wednesday. "That's very unusual, from what I understand."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这非常可爱，他们给了我一枚硬币，”特朗普周三告诉福克斯新闻。 “据我了解，这非常不寻常。”

contentParagraphs.en: A coin with the sitting president's face has only been minted once, exactly a century ago. And, experts say, it's likely illegal.
contentParagraphs.zh: 印有现任总统头像的硬币只铸造过一次，整整一个世纪前。而且，专家表示，这可能是非法的。

contentParagraphs.en: "The law prohibits, currently, on currency, the likeness of any person — not just the president — who is alive," said Jeremy Paul, a professor and former dean at Northeastern University School of Law who specializes in constitutional law.
contentParagraphs.zh: “目前，法律禁止在货币上使用任何在世人的肖像，而不仅仅是总统，”专门研究宪法的东北大学法学院教授、前院长杰里米·保罗说。

contentParagraphs.en: The Treasury Department argues coins do not apply, citing a law that Congress passed in 2020 authorizing the creation of a special-edition 250th coin.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国财政部援引国会 2020 年通过的一项法律，授权发行第 250 枚特别版硬币，认为硬币不适用。

contentParagraphs.en: Paul says the coin question could potentially reach the courts. Either way, he believes, the break with tradition should be cause for concern.
contentParagraphs.zh: 保罗表示，硬币问题可能会诉诸法庭。他认为，无论如何，与传统的决裂应该引起人们的关注。

contentParagraphs.en: "Regardless of careful parsing of the language of these individual statutes, this plan the president and Secretary Bessent have cooked up is inconsistent with the principles of our country, and it politicizes something that's not supposed to be political," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说：“无论仔细解析这些个别法规的语言，总统和贝森特部长制定的这项计划都不符合我们国家的原则，而且它把一些不应该是政治的东西政治化了。”

contentParagraphs.en: President Trump's image has been added to passports, national park passes and federal buildings — like the Department of Justice headquarters — in his second term.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普总统的形象在他的第二个任期内已被添加到护照、国家公园通行证和联邦建筑（如司法部总部）上。

contentParagraphs.en: A Trump $1 coin has been in the works since at least late last year, despite backlash from Democratic lawmakers who introduced a bill to block it. The federal Commission of Fine Arts — which is stacked with presidential appointees — approved the design in March, though it's a different version than the one Treasury officials shared this week.
contentParagraphs.zh: 至少从去年年底开始，一枚特朗普版 1 美元硬币就已经在酝酿之中，尽管民主党议员提出了一项法案来阻止它的强烈反对。由总统任命的联邦美术委员会在三月份批准了这一设计，尽管它与财政部官员本周分享的版本不同。

contentParagraphs.en: This coin, which the Treasury says has a "gold-like finish," will be minted in Philadelphia and available for purchase in the fall.
contentParagraphs.zh: 财政部称这枚硬币具有“类似黄金的外观”，将在费城铸造，并于秋季出售。

contentParagraphs.en: It's one of several ways this administration is seeking to put Trump's mark on American money. In March, the Treasury announced that all future paper currency will bear Trump's signature, another first for a sitting president. It also prepared a $250 bill featuring Trump's face, though Congressional approval — which is required for the new denomination — does not seem imminent.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这是本届政府寻求在美国货币上打上特朗普印记的几种方式之一。今年三月，财政部宣布未来所有纸币都将有特朗普的签名，这对于现任总统来说又是第一次。它还准备了一张印有特朗普头像的 250 美元钞票，尽管国会的批准（新教派需要这一点）似乎不会很快实现。

contentParagraphs.en: And yet, Bessent confirmed last week that his department is no longer planning to move ahead with preexisting efforts to put Harriet Tubman on the $20 bill. That juxtaposition was not lost on Rep. Joyce Beatty, D-Ohio, one of several lawmakers who criticized the new coin from both sides of the aisle.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，贝森特上周证实，他的部门不再计划继续推进先前将哈里特·塔布曼 (Harriet Tubman) 印在 20 美元钞票上的努力。俄亥俄州民主党众议员乔伊斯·比蒂（Joyce Beatty）也没有忽视这种并置，他是两党批评新硬币的几位议员之一。

contentParagraphs.en: "They can put Trump on a $1 coin, but can't keep a decades-old promise to put Harriet Tubman on the $20 bill???" Beatty tweeted. "This Treasury is funding presidential vanity projects."
contentParagraphs.zh: “他们可以把特朗普印在 1 美元的硬币上，但却无法兑现几十年前的承诺，将哈里特·塔布曼印在 20 美元的纸币上？？？”比蒂发推文。 “财政部正在资助总统的虚荣项目。”

contentParagraphs.en: Richard Painter, former chief White House ethics lawyer for President George W. Bush, said the practice of putting the head of state on coins has long been "associated with monarchies and other forms of authoritarian regimes." George Washington actively refused it for this reason, and at least two federal laws forbid it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 乔治·W·布什(George W. Bush)总统的前首席白宫道德律师理查德·佩因特(Richard Painter)表示，将国家元首印在硬币上的做法长期以来“与君主制和其他形式的独裁政权有关”。乔治·华盛顿因此积极拒绝，并且至少有两项联邦法律禁止这样做。

contentParagraphs.en: "One of the reasons for Congress wanting to prohibit this is that we don't want presidents acting like kings," Painter said. "And this is not the only way in which Donald Trump has acted like a king."
contentParagraphs.zh: “国会想要禁止这种行为的原因之一是我们不希望总统表现得像国王，”佩因特说。 “这并不是唐纳德·特朗普表现得像国王的唯一方式。”

contentParagraphs.en: Unpacking the legal questions
contentParagraphs.zh: 解开法律问题

contentParagraphs.en: In the mid-19th century, a Treasury official named Spencer Clark slipped his own portrait on a banknote that Congress had intended to honor the late explorer William Clark (partner to Meriwether Lewis).
contentParagraphs.zh: 19 世纪中叶，一位名叫斯宾塞·克拉克 (Spencer Clark) 的财政部官员将自己的肖像印在国会原本打算纪念已故探险家威廉·克拉克 (William Clark)（梅里韦瑟·刘易斯 (Meriwether Lewis) 的合伙人）的钞票上。

contentParagraphs.en: That prompted outrage, and the Thayer Amendment of 1866. It appears in the U.S. code as: "Only the portrait of a deceased individual may appear on United States currency and securities."
contentParagraphs.zh: 这引起了公愤，并于 1866 年通过了塞耶修正案。它在美国法典中的表述为：“只有已故个人的肖像才可以出现在美国货币和证券上。”

contentParagraphs.en: "We don't want neutral symbols like our money to be partisan, to reflect preference for one living person or another person," Paul explained.
contentParagraphs.zh: 保罗解释说：“我们不希望像我们的钱这样的中立象征带有党派色彩，以反映对一个活着的人或另一个人的偏好。”

contentParagraphs.en: Bessent acknowledged that requirement while showing the coin to Fox News on Tuesday, but argued there is a loophole.
contentParagraphs.zh: 贝森特周二向福克斯新闻展示硬币时承认了这一要求，但认为存在漏洞。

contentParagraphs.en: "During the 150th there was a Calvin Coolidge coin," he said. "So we can put living peoples' images on a coin."
contentParagraphs.zh: “150 年代有一枚卡尔文·柯立芝硬币，”他说。 “这样我们就可以把活着的人的形象印在硬币上。”

contentParagraphs.en: Coolidge was the only president to be featured on a coin in his lifetime, for the sesquicentennial in 1926. That year's celebrations were ultimately labeled a "flop." And demand for the special-edition coin fell vastly short of expectations: over 859,000 pieces out of the 1 million produced were ultimately returned to the Mint and melted.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1926 年，柯立芝是他一生中唯一一位在硬币上出现图案的总统。那一年的庆祝活动最终被贴上了“失败”的标签。对特别版硬币的需求远远低于预期：生产的 100 万枚中，有超过 859,000 枚最终被送回造币厂并熔化。

contentParagraphs.en: "Whatever precedent there might be, the law has changed further in the direction of trying to de-politicize the currency since then," Paul said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 保罗说：“无论有什么先例，从那时起，法律已经朝着试图使货币非政治化的方向进一步改变。”

contentParagraphs.en: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent unveiled the design of the coin this week, defending its legality.
contentParagraphs.zh: 财政部长斯科特·贝森特本周公布了该硬币的设计，捍卫其合法性。

contentParagraphs.en: George Walker IV/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 乔治·沃克四世/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: George Walker IV/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 乔治·沃克四世/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: The 2005 Presidential $1 Coin Act — which authorized a series of coins commemorating past presidents — says a president can only be eligible if they have been deceased for at least two years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2005 年的《1 美元总统硬币法案》授权发行一系列纪念前任总统的硬币，规定总统只有在去世至少两年后才有资格发行。

contentParagraphs.en: The presidential coin program ended in 2016, but released its final coin in 2020 in honor of George H. W. Bush two years after his death.
contentParagraphs.zh: 总统硬币计划于 2016 年结束，但在 2020 年发行了最后一枚硬币，以纪念乔治·H·W·布什去世两年后。

contentParagraphs.en: Also in 2020, during the first Trump administration, Congress passed a law authorizing the treasury secretary to issue $1 coins "with designs emblematic of the United States Semiquincentennial during the one
contentParagraphs.zh: 同样是在 2020 年，在特朗普第一届政府期间，国会通过了一项法律，授权财政部长发行 1 美元硬币，“其设计具有美国五十周年纪念的象征意义”。

articles.title: Second half of summer to bring chances for rain but heatwave threat persists
articles.summary: Forecasts suggest the weather could turn more changeable during late July and August but the risk of heatwaves remains.
articles.titleZh: 夏季下半年将有降雨机会，但热浪威胁依然存在
articles.summaryZh: 天气预报显示，七月下旬和八月天气可能会变得更加多变，但热浪的风险仍然存在。

contentParagraphs.en: Second half of summer to bring chances for rain but heatwave threat persistsImage source, EPA/ShutterstockByBen RichLead Weather PresenterThe UK's weather may turn a little more changeable during the second half of summer with spells of rain featuring in some long-range forecasts.There is also a high chance that temperatures will remain largely above average during late July and August and further heatwaves cannot be ruled out.The season so far has been notable for its heat, with a number of records broken - including some which date back to the notorious summer of 1976.A lack of rain has also been a big part of summer with some places in southern England seeing no measurable rain for more than four weeks.Hosepipe bans are in force for millions of households and dry vegetation has provided fuel for wildfires to burn in a number of areas.Rain may return after prolonged dry spell for manyCurrent trends suggest a chance of more changeable weather during the second half of summer.However, the signals from computer weather models are rather mixed meaning there is a lot of uncertainty.The latest sub-seasonal forecast from DTN - the BBC's weather data supplier - suggests that areas of low pressure will feature more prominently over the next couple of weeks, especially for Scotland and Northern Ireland.This would mean an increase in wet weather.However the Azores high, a semi-permanent large area of high pressure over the North Atlantic which can block weather fronts from reaching the UK, is likely to be close by. This will keep things drier further south, although not necessarily completely so.Temperatures are most likely to remain above the seasonal norm, especially in England and Wales where further hot spells or heatwaves are possible into early August.Image source, Vuk Valcic/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty ImageThe latest Met Office long range forecast hints at similar conditions, suggesting that "the influence of high pressure may wane somewhat" during the final part of July.This would mean an increase in shower and thunderstorm activity, initially in the north of the UK but with rain eventually spreading southwards at times.The Met Office forecast also suggests a changeable start to August, but with above-average temperatures and the chance of further hot spells.Long-range forecasting is fraught with uncertainty.The behaviour of our chaotic atmosphere generally becomes harder to predict the further into the future you gaze.Forecasters are looking for overall trends rather than day-by-day detail and at the moment those trends look rather unclear.Whatever your plans for the rest of the summer, you can always keep up to date with the latest forecasts on the BBC Weather app.A historic summer so farThe summer of 2026 has already been extraordinary.A late-spring heatwave that set a new May temperature record was followed by another heatwave in June, and a third in July which has lasted for two weeks in places.New UK June temperature record: 37.7C at Lingwood, Norfolk, surpassing the previous record of 35.6C from 1976New record for number of days reaching 35C or above: Six days this summer so far, surpassing the previous record of five days set in 1976New record for number of days reaching 34C or above: Nine days this summer so far, surpassing the previous record of eight days set in 1976.By 15 July, the UK had already recorded more days above 30C than in the whole of 1976 - a year that is remembered by many for its iconic and long-lasting heatwave.This year is is also the first on record when 35C or above has been reached in three separate calendar months - May, June and July.What is causing this summer heat?A number of factors have combined to drive the hot weather that we have seen so far this summer.Persistent areas of high pressure have allowed heat to build underneath them, and the lack of rain has allowed the ground to dry out.Dry land encourages higher temperatures because less of the Sun's energy is used in evaporating moisture, leaving more available to heat the ground and the air.Image source, Met Office / BBCImage caption, Mean temperatures - which factor in daytime and overnight values - have been well above average in most parts of the UK over the summer so farSea-surface temperatures around the UK have been much higher than normal, with a so-called marine heatwave continuing around our shores.This reduces the usual cooling effect of the sea and can help to hold temperatures up over land, especially at night.One other key factor is climate change.Scientists are confident that human-induced warming is making heatwaves more frequent and more intense.In its latest State of the UK Climate report, the Met Office says weather that used to be classed as extreme is becoming the "new normal". It says the climate we used to know in the 20th century is now a thing of the past, with the UK warming approximately 0.25C per decade since the 1980s.
contentParagraphs.zh: 夏季后半段可能会下雨，但热浪威胁依然存在 图片来源，EPA/Shutterstock 作者：Ben Rich 天气主播 英国的天气在夏季后半段可能会变得更加多变，一些长期预报中会出现阵雨。7 月下旬和 8 月的气温也很有可能会大大高于平均水平，并且不能排除进一步的热浪。到目前为止，这个季节因其炎热而引人注目，打破了许多记录 - 包括一些可以追溯到很久以前的记录到 1976 年臭名昭著的夏天。缺雨也是夏季的一个重要组成部分，英格兰南部的一些地方已经有超过四个星期没有出现可测量的降雨。数百万户家庭实施了软管禁令，干燥的植被为许多地区的野火提供了燃料。许多地区的长期干旱后，降雨可能会回归。当前的趋势表明，夏季后半段天气可能会更加多变。然而，来自计算机天气模型的信号相当复杂，这意味着有很多不确定性。BBC 天气数据供应商 DTN 的最新次季节预测表明，低压地区将在未来几周内更加突出，特别是苏格兰和北爱尔兰。这意味着潮湿天气会增加。然而，亚速尔高压是北大西洋上空的一个半永久性大面积高压区域，可以阻止锋面到达英国，它很可能就在附近。这将使南部地区变得更加干燥，尽管不一定完全如此。气温很可能保持在季节性正常水平之上，特别是在英格兰和威尔士，那里可能会持续到八月初。图片来源，Vuk Valcic/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Image最新的英国气象局长期预报暗示了类似的情况，表明“高压的影响可能会在七月下旬有所减弱”。这意味着阵雨和雷暴活动将会增加，最初在英国北部，但有时降雨最终会向南蔓延。英国气象局的预测还表明，八月开始会发生变化，但气温高于平均水平，并且可能会出现进一步的高温天气。长期预测充满了不确定性。随着您对未来的展望，我们混乱的大气层的行为通常会变得更难预测。预报员正在寻找总体趋势，而不是逐日细节，目前这些趋势看起来相当不清楚。无论您对今年夏天剩余时间的计划是什么，您可以随时了解 BBC 天气应用程序的最新预报。迄今为止历史性的夏天 2026 年的夏天已经非同寻常。春末热浪创下了 5 月气温新纪录，随后 6 月又迎来了另一场热浪，7 月又出现了第三次热浪，在一些地方持续了两周。英国 6 月气温新纪录：诺福克郡灵伍德的气温为 37.7 摄氏度，超过了 1976 年创下的 35.6 摄氏度的记录天数达到新纪录35 摄氏度或以上：今年夏天迄今有 6 天，超过了 1976 年创下的 5 天记录。达到 34 摄氏度或以上的天数新纪录：今年夏天迄今有 9 天，超过了 1976 年创下的 8 天记录。到 7 月 15 日，英国记录的 30 摄氏度以上天数已超过 1976 年全年，这一年因其标志性且持久的热浪而被许多人铭记。今年也是有记录以来，在三个不同的日历月（五月、六月和七月）中，气温首次达到 35 摄氏度或以上。造成夏季炎热的原因是什么？许多因素综合在一起，导致了今年夏天迄今为止我们所看到的炎热天气。持续的高压区域使热量在其下方积聚，而缺乏降雨又使地面变得干燥。干燥的土地导致气温升高，因为用于蒸发水分的太阳能量较少，从而有更多的能量可用于加热地面和空气。图片来源，英国气象局/BBC 图片说明，迄今为止，英国大部分地区的平均气温（考虑白天和夜间的数值）远高于平均水平。英国周围的海面温度远高于正常水平，所谓的海洋热浪持续在我们的海岸周围。这减少了海洋通常的冷却效果，有助于保持陆地温度升高，特别是在夜间。另一个关键因素是气候变化。科学家相信，人类引起的变暖正在使热浪变得更加频繁和频繁。英国气象局在最新的英国气候报告中表示，过去被归类为极端的天气正在成为“新常态”。报告称，我们在 20 世纪所熟悉的气候现已成为过去，自 20 世纪 80 年代以来，英国每十年变暖约 0.25 摄氏度。

articles.title: World Cup final: Spain vs Argentina – Which team has best chance to win?
articles.summary: What to know about both teams: Form, strengths, weaknesses, key players and what the coaches and players had to say.
articles.titleZh: 世界杯决赛：西班牙 vs 阿根廷 – 哪支球队最有机会获胜？
articles.summaryZh: 关于两支球队的了解：状态、优势、劣势、关键球员以及教练和球员的评价。

contentParagraphs.en: The 48-team FIFA World Cup 2026 tournament now comes down to one game: Spain against Argentina.It is hard to argue that the European champions and world number one team taking on the South American champions and World Cup holders is not a fitting finale.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4How World Cup football jerseys became political fashion in Latin Americalist 2 of 4World Cup: All to know before Sunday’s Spain vs Argentina blockbuster finallist 3 of 4FIFA World Cup 2026: Biggest takeaways from the semifinalslist 4 of 4‘We are unique’: How Argentina overcame England to reach World Cup finalend of listFrance had stormed into the pole position as favourites during the tournament, while England seemed to have their best chance since their only win 60 years ago.Nevertheless, there is plenty to pick through when it comes to the finalists, so Al Jazeera does exactly that:Argentina – World Cup holders and South American championsPrevious World Cup appearances: 18 Best performance: Winners (1978, 1986, 2022) First appearance: 1930 (Uruguay) Overall World Cup record: P94 W53 D17 L24 F171 A103 FIFA world ranking: 3Overview: If their 2022 challenge in Qatar got off to a shocking start, with defeat by Saudi Arabia, then the 2026 campaign for Argentina could be seen as a flyer in comparison.The truth, however, is that this has been far from a vintage Argentina, especially with Lionel Messi still the pivot of the team. The 39-year-old is, nevertheless, the reason that the title defence is ongoing.The last side to win back-to-back World Cups was Brazil in 1958 and 1962. Italy have also retained a title, so it is a very short list that Argentina are seeking to join.Strengths: Look no further than Messi for Argentina’s clear and unmatched – at least at this tournament – strength. The Inter Miami forward is set to win the Golden Boot with eight goals. The midfield trio holding in front of the back line: Enzo Fernandes, Alexis Mac Allister and Paredes, have been seen as the enforcers of the tournament, protecting Messi as much as the back line; they have even been compared to personal bodyguards for the Argentina legend.Weaknesses: Due to the weight of reliance on Messi, Argentina’s weakness has to be their ability to replace their main man. Should Messi sustain an injury, Argentina’s odds – already wide – would stretch further. They have pace and guile in Giuliano Simeone and Julian Alvarez, and goals in Lautaro Martinez; they also have power in their defence and holding midfielders, but do they have a spark without Messi? Arguably, no team at World Cup 2026 has been more reliant on one player.Form guide: Argentina have not lost a match since they were beaten 1-0 in Ecuador in a FIFA World Cup qualifying match. Since then, the South American champions have gone on a 14-game winning streak.The Argentinians rattled in eight goals as they swept the group stage of the World Cup, conceding only once – in their final game against Jordan when already qualified for the round of 32.The knockouts have proved far trickier, with 3-2 wins recorded in the last 32 and last 16, against Cape Verde and Egypt, respectively.Their progress from the quarterfinals was secured by a 3-1 win against Switzerland, but that game – much like the Cape Verde win – required extra time.The 2-1 semifinal win against England saw a second great turnaround at the tournament, having trailed the Egyptians 2-0 with 11 minutes of the 90 remaining.Players to watch: Argentina not only threw the kitchen sink at England, but they also threw the extent of their attacking talent that started on the bench.Along with Lautaro Martinez, coach Lionel Scaloni threw on Rodrigo De Paul and Nicolas Gonzalez. Although Messi is irreplaceable, the Argentinians are likely to need the full extent of the resources around him if they are to claim the win.At the other end, Lisandro Martinez remains the main man at the heart of the defence.Spain – European champions and world’s number one teamPrevious World Cup appearances: 17 Best finish: Winners (2010) Overall World Cup record: P74 W37 D18 L19 F121 A76 FIFA ranking: 1Overview: Spain Spain have recovered from two terrible World Cup campaigns – having not reached the quarterfinals at the last two editions – to have the chance of becoming the reigning European and World champions for the second time.The Spanish lifted the global title for the only time in their history in 2014, two years after their first continental crown.Their number one ranking by FIFA on entering the tournament is in large part based on the victory at Euro 2024.Strengths: Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams, now 19 and 24, respectively, were the rising stars of Spain’s continental win in 2024, but neither has fired at the World Cup.Yamal’s fitness entering the tournament was a concern, given he missed the end of the domestic Spanish season with a hamstring injury, while Williams has mostly been restricted to cameos from the bench in North America.As lauded as Argentina’s defence has been, Spain have only conceded one goal at the tournament so far. Their organisation as a nation, be it at club or international level, is rarely anything other than immaculate.The rapid, short passing game “Tiki-taki” started in Spain during Pep Guardiola’s time with Barcelona – ironically, when Messi was leading the line for the Catalan club and was the main reason for its success.The Spaniards will once again look for their heavy possession-based game to win the day, with Rodri and Fabian Ruiz acting as the fulcrums in midfield.Weaknesses: As in most walks of life, a strength can also be a weakness. Where question marks have been raised about Spain at this edition, it has mostly surrounded a pedestrian pace.The short passing game remains, but it has not been as swift and effective this time around, especially against the low block – something they are likely to face against Argentina.Most of the sides have set up that way against the Spanish so far, with Cape V
contentParagraphs.zh: 48 队参加的 2026 年 FIFA 世界杯锦标赛现在归结为一场比赛：西班牙对阵阿根廷。很难说，欧洲冠军和世界排名第一的球队对阵南美冠军和世界杯卫冕冠军并不是一个合适的结局。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 世界杯足球球衣如何成为拉丁美洲的政治时尚列表 2 of 4 世界杯：在周日的西班牙对阵阿根廷重磅决赛列表 3 之前都知道4 国际足联 2026 年世界杯：半决赛的最大收获 名单 4 中的 4 “我们是独一无二的”：阿根廷如何击败英格兰进入世界杯决赛 法国在比赛期间以夺冠热门的身份冲进了杆位，而英格兰似乎拥有自 60 年前唯一一场胜利以来的最佳机会。尽管如此，在决赛入围者方面仍有很多值得挑选的地方，所以半岛电视台正是这么做的：阿根廷 - 世界杯卫冕者和南美冠军 历届世界杯出场次数：18 最佳表现：获胜者（1978 年、1986 年、2022 年） 首次亮相：1930 年（乌拉圭） 世界杯总战绩：P94 W53 D17 L24 F171 A103 国际足联世界排名：3 概述：如果他们 2022 年在卡塔尔的挑战开局令人震惊，被沙特阿拉伯击败，那么 2026 年的世界杯相比之下，阿根廷可以被视为飞人。然而，事实是，这远非老式的阿根廷，尤其是在莱昂内尔·梅西仍然是球队的中枢的情况下。尽管如此，这位39岁的球员仍然是卫冕之路仍在继续的原因。最后一支连续两次赢得世界杯冠军的球队是1958年和1962年的巴西队。意大利也卫冕了冠军，因此阿根廷寻求加入的名单非常短。 优势：阿根廷队至少在本届世界杯上明显且无与伦比的实力，不用再看梅西了。这位国际迈阿密前锋有望以八个进球赢得金靴奖。中场三人组在后防线前站稳脚跟：恩佐·费尔南德斯、亚历克西斯·麦克·阿利斯特和帕雷德斯，被视为本届赛事的执行者，他们保护梅西和后防线一样；他们甚至被比作阿根廷传奇人物的私人保镖。 弱点：由于对梅西的依赖，阿根廷队的弱点就是他们更换主力球员的能力。如果梅西受伤，阿根廷队本已很大的胜算将进一步扩大。他们拥有朱利亚诺·西蒙尼和朱利安·阿尔瓦雷斯的速度和智慧，以及劳塔罗·马丁内斯的进球；他们在防守和控球方面也有实力，但如果没有梅西，他们还有火花吗？可以说，2026 年世界杯上没有哪支球队比这支球队更依赖一名球员。 状态指南：自从在 FIFA 世界杯预选赛中以 1-0 击败厄瓜多尔以来，阿根廷队还没有输过一场比赛。此后，这支南美冠军队已经取得了 14 场连胜。阿根廷队在世界杯小组赛中横扫对手，打入 8 粒进球，只丢了一次——在已经晋级 32 强的最后一场对阵约旦的比赛中。事实证明，淘汰赛要棘手得多，32 强和 16 强分别对阵佛得角和埃及，淘汰赛成绩为 3-2。 3-1 战胜瑞士，但那场比赛——就像战胜佛得角一样——需要加时赛。半决赛 2-1 战胜英格兰队，这是本届赛事的第二次伟大逆转，在比赛还剩 90 分钟的情况下，埃及队以 2-0 落后。值得关注的球员：阿根廷队不仅把英格兰队打得一败涂地，而且还展示了他们从替补席上开始的进攻天赋。除了劳塔罗·马丁内斯，主教练莱昂内尔·斯卡罗尼也表现出色。罗德里戈·德保罗和尼古拉斯·冈萨雷斯。尽管梅西是不可替代的，但阿根廷人如果想赢得胜利，可能需要他身边的全部资源。另一方面，利桑德罗·马丁内斯仍然是后防线的核心人物。西班牙 – 欧洲冠军和世界第一球队历次世界杯出场次数：17 最佳成绩：优胜者（2010） 世界杯总战绩：P74 W37 D18 L19 F121 A76 国际足联排名： 1 概述：西班牙 西班牙已经从两届糟糕的世界杯征程中恢复过来——过去两届都没有进入四分之一决赛——有机会第二次成为卫冕欧洲和世界冠军。西班牙队在 2014 年夺得了他们历史上唯一的一次全球冠军，距离他们第一次夺得大陆冠军两年后。国际足联在进入世界杯时的排名在很大程度上是基于 2024 年欧洲杯的胜利。优势：拉明·亚马尔和尼科·威廉姆斯，现在排名第19位和第24位的两位分别是西班牙2024年欧洲大陆夺冠的后起之秀，但两人都没有在世界杯上火力全开。亚马尔因腿筋受伤缺席了西班牙国内赛季的尾声，因此进入世界杯时的健康状况令人担忧，而威廉姆斯在北美的比赛中大多只能在替补席上客串。尽管阿根廷的防守备受赞誉，但西班牙迄今为止在世界杯上只丢了一球。他们作为一个国家的组织，无论是在俱乐部还是国际层面，都堪称完美。快速、短传的“Tiki-taki”比赛在瓜迪奥拉效力巴塞罗那期间在西班牙开始——讽刺的是，当时梅西在加泰罗尼亚俱乐部中处于领先地位，也是其成功的主要原因。西班牙人将再次寻求以控球为基础的比赛来赢得胜利，罗德里和法比安·鲁伊斯将成为球队的支点。中场。弱点：与大多数行业一样，优势也可能是弱点。在这一届比赛中，人们对西班牙提出了疑问，但大多都是围绕着步行的节奏。短传比赛仍然存在，但这次没有那么迅速和有效，特别是在面对低位阻挡时——他们很可能会在对阵阿根廷时遇到这种情况。到目前为止，大多数球队都以这种方式对阵西班牙队，Cape V

articles.title: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
articles.summary: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普媒体将出售“影响市场”社交帖子的即时访问权限
articles.summaryZh: 特朗普媒体正在为华尔街交易员推出其最有影响力的帖子的快速付费提要。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump Media & Technology Group, which owns Truth Social, is launching a paid service to give Wall Street firms high-speed access to its most influential posts.From 1 August, instant updates will be delivered from "the highest-ranking" accounts, it said. US President Donald Trump currently has the most followers on the platform.The company behind the app hopes it will create a steady new source of money for the firm which is currently loss-making.It is likely to be aimed at financial traders who want to see market-moving news fast. Trump’s social media posts often cause sudden swings in global markets, especially when he writes about trade and tariffs.For firms, a delay of even seconds can be costly. Until now, banks and traders had to monitor the app manually. The new system will send posts directly to paying clients. "Markets already move on Truth Social posts," said Kevin McGurn, the interim boss of Trump Media, adding that the service will create a steady profit. Trump Media did not say how much it would charge for the service.The new commercial data feed, named Truth API, promises to deliver posts to paying institutional clients in "milliseconds". The service will run 24 hours a day, seven days a week.The company, which launched its social media app in 2022, said some firms have been copying its data for months without permission.McGurn warned that Trump Media will soon block these methods, forcing firms to buy the official feed instead. Trump could benefit substantially from the move. Since his family remains the majority shareholder in the company, the president stands to profit directly from selling expedited access to his own public statements.The BBC has contacted Trump Media for comment on whether or not the president's posts will be included in the paid feed. The White House declined to comment.While other social media networks already sell data, the move highlights the unique overlap between Trump’s private businesses and his public role as president.It would be "unprecedented" if the feature did include the president's posts, said Mark Spiegel, an investment expert from Stanphyl Capital Management.Companies that trade off the latest headlines would be "at a disadvantage" if they did not pay for quick access as they could miss out on posts that could move markets, Spiegel said."But to put this in context, remember that Trump's posts constitute just a tiny fraction of what moves markets," he added.Robert Frenchman, a partner at US law firm Dynamis, told Reuters: "It certainly does not seem fair, but yes, a tech platform can tier its distribution of information without violating federal securities laws."Additional reporting by Osmond Chia
contentParagraphs.zh: 拥有 Truth Social 的特朗普媒体与科技集团正在推出一项付费服务​​，让华尔街公司能够快速访问其最具影响力的帖子。该公司表示，从 8 月 1 日起，“排名最高”的账户将提供即时更新。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普目前在该平台上拥有最多的追随者。该应用程序背后的公司希望它能为目前亏损的公司创造稳定的新资金来源。它很可能针对希望快速查看市场动态新闻的金融交易员。特朗普的社交媒体帖子经常会引起全球市场的突然波动，尤其是当他撰写有关贸易和关税的文章时。对于企业来说，即使是几秒钟的延迟也可能造成高昂的代价。到目前为止，银行和交易员必须手动监控该应用程序。新系统将直接向付费客户发送帖子。特朗普媒体临时老板凯文·麦格恩表示：“市场已经开始关注真相社交帖子。”他补充说，该服务将创造稳定的利润。特朗普媒体没有透露这项服务的收费标准。新的商业数据源名为 Truth API，承诺在​​“毫秒”内向付费机构客户发送帖子。该服务将每周 7 天、每天 24 小时运行。该公司于 2022 年推出了社交媒体应用程序，表示一些公司几个月来一直在未经许可的情况下复制其数据。麦格恩警告说，特朗普媒体将很快阻止这些方法，迫使公司转而购买官方源。特朗普可能会从此举中获益匪浅。由于他的家族仍然是该公司的大股东，总统可以通过出售他自己的公开声明的快速访问权来直接获利。英国广播公司（BBC）已联系特朗普媒体，请其就总统的帖子是否会包含在付费提要中发表评论。白宫拒绝发表评论。虽然其他社交媒体网络已经出售数据，但此举突显了特朗普的私营企业与他作为总统的公共角色之间的独特重叠。斯坦菲尔资本管理公司的投资专家马克·斯皮格尔表示，如果该功能确实包含总统的帖子，那将是“前所未有的”。斯皮格尔表示，如果不付费快速访问，权衡最新头条新闻的公司将“处于不利地位”，因为他们可能会错过可能影响市场的帖子。结合上下文来看，请记住，特朗普的帖子只占推动市场的一小部分。”他补充道。美国Dynamis律师事务所合伙人罗伯特·弗兰西曼(Robert Frenchman)对路透社表示：“这当然看起来不公平，但是，是的，科技平台可以在不违反联邦证券法的情况下分级其信息分发。”

articles.title: Brewdog founder faces data complaints over efforts to buy back firm
articles.summary: The UK data watchdog has received complaints after James Watt reportedly contacted former shareholders.
articles.titleZh: Brewdog 创始人因回购公司的努力而面临数据投诉
articles.summaryZh: 据报道，詹姆斯·瓦特联系前股东后，英国数据监管机构收到了投诉。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, PA MediaImage caption, James Watt has made a bid to buy back the Scottish craft beer firmBrewdog co-founder James Watt is facing complaints to the UK data watchdog after he reportedly contacted former shareholders as part of efforts to take back control of the craft brewer.The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) said it was "assessing" information following complaints related to Watt.Earlier this year, US drinks firm Tilray took over Brewdog in a deal worth about £33m after the company collapsed with debts of more than £500m, leading to hundreds of job losses.The administration process saw Brewdog shut 36 bars while the takeover deal rendered the shares of about 200,000 crowdfunding investors worthless.Tilray bought Brewdog's brand, intellectual property, UK breweries and 11 bars.Watt stepped down as chief executive of the brand in 2024 to become its "captain and co-founder".On Wednesday, Watt made a bid to buy back the Scottish craft beer firm just months after it was acquired.He tabled an offer to buy Brewdog through his new beer firm, Second Best, claiming that 43,000 so-called equity punk investors had joined forces for the bid.The Guardian has reported, external that a number of shareholders contacted by Watt said they did not understand how he had their contact details, raising concerns about a potential breach of the general data protection regulation (GDPR).The data privacy rules are overseen by the ICO."We are aware of an incident involving Brewdog and we are assessing the information provided," an ICO spokeswoman said.Watt has been contacted for comment.He co-founded Brewdog with Martin Dickie in Aberdeenshire in 2007.At its peak, the company had four breweries, about 100 pubs worldwide and was said to be worth more than $1bn.Image source, Getty ImagesEarlier this week, bosses at Tilray stressed that the brand was not for sale and planned to reject Watt's takeover efforts.A spokesman added: "Tilray Brands did not acquire Equity for Punk shareholder data as part of its acquisition of the BrewDog brand and assets; that records system remains under the control of BrewDog plc (in administration)."Tilray acquired only a customer CRM database comprising individuals who explicitly opted in to BrewDog communications."For the avoidance of doubt, Tilray Brands (trading as BrewDog) and its current management team have no involvement in, affiliation with, or responsibility for James Watt's business activities, including Second Best."The spokesman added that Tilray Brands did not "authorise, facilitate, or participate in the communications reportedly sent to former Equity for Punks investors and did not authorise the use of any acquired data for such purposes"."We take data privacy with the utmost seriousness and can categorically confirm that no data held by Tilray Brands has been shared with external entities or former directors," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片来源，PA Media 图片说明，詹姆斯·瓦特 (James Watt) 已出价回购苏格兰精酿啤酒公司 Brewdog 联合创始人詹姆斯·瓦特 (James Watt) 正面临英国数据监管机构的投诉，据报道，他联系了前股东，作为夺回精酿啤酒制造商控制权的一部分。信息专员办公室 (ICO) 表示，正在“评估”与瓦特相关的投诉后的信息。 今年早些时候，美国饮料公司 Tilray 以价值约 3300 万英镑的交易收购了 Brewdog该公司负债超过 5 亿英镑，倒闭，导致数百人失业。管理程序导致 Brewdog 关闭了 36 家酒吧，而收购交易使约 20 万众筹投资者的股票变得一文不值。Tilray 收购了 Brewdog 的品牌、知识产权、英国啤酒厂和 11 家酒吧。瓦特于 2024 年辞去该品牌首席执行官一职，成为其“队长和联合创始人”。周三，瓦特就在这家苏格兰精酿啤酒公司被收购几个月后，他提出了回购 Brewdog 的要约，并声称有 43,000 名所谓的股权朋克投资者联合起来参与了此次收购。 据《卫报》报道，瓦特外部联系的一些股东表示，他们不明白他是如何获得自己的联系方式的，这引发了人们对可能违反一般数据保护条例 (GDPR) 的担忧。 数据隐私规则由ICO。 ICO 发言人表示：“我们知道涉及 Brewdog 的事件，正在评估所提供的信息。”我们已联系瓦特征求意见。他于 2007 年与 Martin Dickie 在阿伯丁郡共同创立了 Brewdog。在鼎盛时期，该公司在全球拥有四家啤酒厂，约 100 家酒吧，据说价值超过 10 亿美元。图片来源：Getty Images 本周早些时候，Tilray 的老板强调，该品牌不打算出售，并计划拒绝 Watt 的收购努力。一位发言人补充道：“作为收购 BrewDog 品牌和资产的一部分，Tilray Brands 并未收购 Equity for Punk 股东数据；该记录系统仍由 BrewDog plc（管理中）控制。”Tilray 仅收购了一个客户 CRM 数据库，其中包含明确选择加入 BrewDog 通信的个人。 BrewDog）及其现任管理团队不参与、隶属 James Watt 的商业活动，包括 Second Best。发言人补充说，Tilray Brands 并未“授权、促进或参与据报道发送给 Equity for Punks 前投资者的通信，也没有授权将任何获得的数据用于此类目的”。外部实体或前董事，”他说。

articles.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
articles.summary: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.
articles.titleZh: 这座沙漠城市可以教给世界什么来解决热死问题
articles.summaryZh: 亚利桑那州马里科帕县的气温最高，但它减少了与高温相关的死亡人数。

contentParagraphs.en: It has been a blisteringly hot summer for much of the US and Europe.Scientists have warned that these extreme temperatures are the new normal for these communities.France reported an increase of more than 2,000 deaths during the record-breaking heatwave in late June. In England and Wales, scientists estimated that more than 2,700 people may have died from heat-related causes from a series of heatwaves since May. And in the US, at least 44 heat-related deaths were reported over the 4 July holiday weekend from the extreme weather. For many, the rising temperatures have been a shock to the system, a new reality people have had to adjust to as extreme weather events become more frequent and intense with climate change."People don't realise this is not the same heat that we were experiencing 10 years ago, it is actually worse, because in many cases nighttime temperatures are not cooling off," Jennifer Marlon, a Yale University researcher who looks at the impacts of extreme heat, told the BBC.Our bodies rely on cooler nighttime temperatures to recover from the heat of the day.But for one city in the US, hot temperatures are not new. Officials in Phoenix, Arizona, have spent years working on solutions to reduce heat deaths, an effort that seems to be working, and could provide a blueprint for the world. Maricopa County where Phoenix is located, has some of the hottest temperatures in the US so it finds itself at the forefront of the battle over heat safety - working on programmes that offer residents access to cooling centres and free air conditioning. The county's efforts to reduce the number of heat-related deaths have proved successful in recent years. After heat-related deaths reached a peak of 645 in 2023, they declined to 405 in 2025 - with many experts attributing part of the decline to the policy changes. Much of the county's focus has been on increasing access to air conditioning, as many heat deaths were among people with low incomes or experiencing homelessness.The county offers consistent, open access to cool spaces. They have expanded the hours those spaces are open - in some cases they are open 24 hours - getting people off the streets and out of the heat. Another programme offers eligible residents repairs or replacements to their air conditioning systems. "The world has a lot to learn from Maricopa County," Marlon says.But the downward trend is not guaranteed. Already this year, as of 11 July, Maricopa County has recorded 23 heat-related deaths, with another 282 more under investigation. Should those hold, the numbers would outpace the previous year. Ladd Keith, director of the Heat Resilience Initiative at the University of Arizona, told the BBC that other places could, like Phoenix, appoint a heat officer - someone to oversee initiatives and run communications among government offices."It's incredibly important to make someone responsible for heat because the problem is, if it's no one's responsibility, then no one will address it," he says.With coordination and communication, facilities like cooling centres can be fashioned in other parts of the US not accustomed to the scorching heat, Marlon says.But that takes widespread acknowledgement and understanding of how serious heat can be and that it is not temporary, experts say.Heatwaves have become more frequent, more intense and longer-lasting because of human-induced climate change. The world has already warmed by more than 1.1C since the industrial era began and temperatures will keep rising for some time even if governments around the world make steep cuts to emissions.It is also essential that cities and jurisdictions understand that heat is not only a health issue but an infrastructure and economic issue as well, according to experts.Roads will continue to buckle and flights will continue to be delayed from extreme heat, Marlon warns.But most of all, Keith says, it's imperative people understand this oppressive heat will continue."We have to shift away from planning for the historic heat we've experienced and start to plan for the heat we're going to experience in the next five to 10 years," he says."If people think it's bad now, it's going to be hotter, and it's going to be hotter longer. The records are going to be broken, you can almost guarantee, year after year across the world."
contentParagraphs.zh: 对于美国和欧洲的大部分地区来说，这是一个酷热的夏季。科学家警告说，这些极端气温已成为这些社区的新常态。法国报告称，在 6 月底破纪录的热浪中，死亡人数增加了 2,000 多人。在英格兰和威尔士，科学家估计自 5 月份以来一系列热浪造成的热浪可能导致超过 2,700 人死亡。在美国，7 月 4 日的假日周末，据报道至少有 44 人因极端天气而死亡。对许多人来说，气温上升对系统造成了冲击，这是随着气候变化导致极端天气事件变得更加频繁和严重，人们不得不适应的新现实。“人们没有意识到这与我们十年前经历的高温不同，实际上更糟，因为在许多情况下夜间温度并没有降温，”耶鲁大学研究极端高温影响的研究员詹妮弗·马龙告诉英国广播公司。但对于美国的一个城市来说，高温并不新鲜。亚利桑那州凤凰城的官员花了数年时间研究减少热死亡的解决方案，这一努力似乎正在发挥作用，并可以为世界提供蓝图。菲尼克斯所在的马里科帕县是美国气温最高的县之一，因此该县处于热安全斗争的最前沿，正在开展为居民提供冷却中心和免费空调的项目。近年来，该县减少高温死亡人数的努力已被证明是成功的。 2023 年，与高温相关的死亡人数达到峰值 645 人，到 2025 年，死亡人数下降至 405 人，许多专家将死亡人数下降的部分原因归因于政策的变化。该县的重点主要放在增加空调的使用上，因为许多热死者都是低收入或无家可归的人。该县提供持续、开放的凉爽空间。他们延长了这些空间的开放时间——在某些情况下，24 小时开放——让人们远离街道，远离炎热。另一项计划为符合条件的居民提供空调系统的维修或更换服务。马龙说：“世界有很多东西可以向马里科帕县学习。”但下降趋势并不能保证。今年，截至 7 月 11 日，马里科帕县已记录 23 例与高温相关的死亡事件，另有 282 例正在接受调查。如果这些保持不变，这一数字将超过前一年。亚利桑那大学耐热计划主任拉德·基思告诉英国广播公司，其他地方可以像菲尼克斯一样任命一名高温官员，负责监督倡议并负责政府办公室之间的沟通。他说：“让专人负责高温问题非常重要，因为问题是，如果没有人负责，那么就没有人会解决它。”通过协调和沟通，可以在美国其他不习惯酷热的地方建造像冷却中心这样的设施。马龙说。但是，专家表示，这需要人们广泛认识和理解高温的严重程度，而且它不是暂时的。由于人类引起的气候变化，热浪变得更加频繁、更加强烈和持久。自工业时代开始以来，全球气温已经升高了 1.1 摄氏度以上，即使世界各国政府大幅削减排放量，气温仍将在一段时间内持续上升。专家表示，城市和司法管辖区也必须认识到高温不仅是一个健康问题，而且还是一个基础设施和经济问题。马龙警告说，道路将继续因酷热而弯曲，航班将继续延误。但最重要的是，基思说，人们必须了解这种令人难以忍受的高温将持续下去。 “我们必须放弃为我们经历过的历史性高温做好准备，而开始为我们将在未来五到十年内经历的高温做好准备。”他说。“如果人们认为现在天气很糟糕，那么天气将会更热，而且会更热，而且几乎可以保证，世界各地的记录将会被打破。”

articles.summary: Palestine football fans honour Egyptian coach Hossam Hassan
