articles.title: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
articles.summary: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.
articles.titleZh: 由于美国的限制措施挤压了中国的技术准入，中国习近平呼吁加大全球在人工智能领域的努力
articles.summaryZh: 美国主导的限制措施阻止了中国获得一些世界上最先进的技术，刺激了中国努力建立自己的专有技术，并加剧了世界两大经济体之间的科技竞赛。

contentParagraphs.en: Chinese President Xi Jinping waves as he arrives at the opening ceremony for the World AI Conference in Shanghai, Friday, July 17, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 7 月 17 日星期五，中国国家主席习近平抵达上海世界人工智能大会开幕式时挥手致意。

contentParagraphs.en: Ng Han Guan/AP Pool
contentParagraphs.zh: 韩官/美联社报道

contentParagraphs.en: Ng Han Guan/AP Pool
contentParagraphs.zh: 韩官/美联社报道

contentParagraphs.en: SHANGHAI — Development and governance of artificial intelligence should be a global effort, Chinese President Xi Jinping said Friday, while reiterating China's objections to what he called the "overstretching" of national security concerns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 上海——中国国家主席习近平周五表示，人工智能的开发和治理应该是全球的共同努力，同时重申中国反对他所说的“过度扩张”国家安全问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Speaking at a conference in Shanghai, Xi said AI should not be dominated by any single nation. American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.
contentParagraphs.zh: 习近平在上海的一次会议上发表讲话时表示，人工智能不应由任何一个国家主导。美国主导的限制措施阻止了中国获得一些世界上最先进的技术，刺激了中国努力建立自己的专有技术，并加剧了世界两大经济体之间的科技竞赛。

contentParagraphs.en: "The development of artificial intelligence should not be a solo performance by any single country but rather a symphony of global cooperation," Xi said at China's annual World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai. Others attending included the leaders of Kazakhstan, Cambodia and Thailand and U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres.
contentParagraphs.zh: 习近平在上海举行的中国年度世界人工智能大会上表示：“人工智能的发展不应是任何一个国家的独奏，而是全球合作的交响乐。”其他出席者包括哈萨克斯坦、柬埔寨和泰国领导人以及联合国秘书长安东尼奥·古特雷斯。

contentParagraphs.en: "We should together oppose the practice of overstretching the concept of national security in the field of artificial intelligence, and of placing one's own security above that of other countries," he said, repeating a longstanding Chinese complaint.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们应该共同反对在人工智能领域过度延伸国家安全概念、将自身安全置于别国安全之上的做法，”他重申了中国长期以来的抱怨。

contentParagraphs.en: Over the next five years, he said China will provide 5,000 training opportunities on artificial intelligence to developing countries.
contentParagraphs.zh: 未来5年，中国将为发展中国家提供5000个人工智能培训名额。

contentParagraphs.en: China will expand AI cooperation with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, the League of Arab States, the African Union, the Community of Latin American and Caribbean States, the Shanghai Cooperation Organization and the BRICS countries, Xi said. He promised to provide access for 30 countries to a Chinese-developed AI meteorological system that provides early warning systems.
contentParagraphs.zh: 习近平表示，中国将扩大同东南亚国家联盟、阿拉伯国家联盟、非盟、拉美和加勒比国家共同体、上海合作组织和金砖国家的人工智能合作。他承诺为30个国家提供中国开发的人工智能气象系统，该系统可提供预警系统。

contentParagraphs.en: A day earlier, 29 countries including Pakistan, Russia and Kazakhstan signed an agreement with China to establish a World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization. State media described it as an intergovernmental organization headquartered in Shanghai promoting global AI governance.
contentParagraphs.zh: 此前一天，巴基斯坦、俄罗斯、哈萨克斯坦等29个国家与中国签署建立世界人工智能合作组织协议。官方媒体将其描述为一个总部位于上海的政府间组织，旨在促进全球人工智能治理。

contentParagraphs.en: More than 1,100 companies and 1,400 guests are participating in the annual AI conference this year, state media said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据官方媒体报道，今年的年度人工智能大会有超过 1,100 家公司和 1,400 名嘉宾参加。

contentParagraphs.en: During the conference, tech giant Huawei will be showcasing its powerful AI computing system, the Atlas 950 SuperPoD,.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大会期间，科技巨头华为将展示其强大的AI计算系统Atlas 950 SuperPoD。

contentParagraphs.en: Some technology analysts now believe China has become an innovator in AI and is no longer just catching up with the U.S. China's five-year plan until 2030 has prioritized progress in frontiers of science and technology including AI.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些科技分析师现在认为，中国已经成为人工智能领域的创新者，不再只是追赶美国。中国到2030年的五年计划优先发展包括人工智能在内的科技前沿领域。

contentParagraphs.en: China's open-source AI models, like DeepSeek, have been seen as appealing and often times more affordable alternatives globally to U.S. AI models, which are largely closed-source, and especially across the developing world.
contentParagraphs.zh: DeepSeek 等中国的开源人工智能模型在全球范围内被视为比美国人工智能模型更有吸引力且价格更实惠的替代品，美国人工智能模型基本上是闭源的，尤其是在发展中国家。

articles.title: The financial winners and losers from the World Cup
articles.summary: Big bucks are being made from the 2026 tournament off the field, but who is raking in the most, and who is losing out?
articles.titleZh: 世界杯的经济赢家和输家
articles.summaryZh: 2026 年锦标赛在场外赚得盆满钵满，但谁赚得最多，谁又输了呢？

contentParagraphs.en: While broadcasters have had to spend a fortune to televise the tournament, the viewing figures - and sponsors wanting their brands on show - mean they are also likely to make a killing in selling advertising slots.Fifa brought in the much-talked about hydration breaks for this World Cup - a move Infantino said was "purely a sporting matter" with no additional revenue for the governing body.However, the three minutes for players to get fluids on board has provided a new commercial opportunity for broadcasters and sponsors.Fox Sports, who reportedly paid $485m for the US broadcast rights, introduced the breaks as "sponsored by" a brand.According to experts, an average 30-second World Cup advertising slot on Fox costs between $200,000 and $300,000. It reached as high as $750,000 during US matches the final stages."The hydration breaks are pure advertising inventory. I'd be extremely surprised if they disappear. The expanded format will stay because scale is now Fifa's business model," says Laboure of Deutsche Bank Research.Fans in the UK watching games on the BBC or ITV have been shielded from hydration break adverts due to the former not using any advertising and the latter being restricted by regulator rules on the amount of commercials used in a 60-minute period.The official sponsors of the World Cup pay eye-watering sums to associate brands with the competition, but no doubt end up benefiting financially, with the likes of Adidas and Coca-Cola plastered everywhere. The German sportswear brand has been locked in a battle with its arch-rival Nike, spending some £50m on their "backyard legends" ad featuring Lamine Yamal, Jude Bellingham and Lionel Messi.However, some unofficial brands have also benefited from Fifa trying to make sure fans see less of them, such as the Levi's logo outside the Levi's stadium in San Francisco being covered up.The 16 host cities across the US, Canada and Mexico have been welcoming an influx of fans and tourists boosting hospitality, hotels and local businesses. But while the Scots drank Boston dry and have won the heart of the city and its people, experts say the long-term economic benefits are minimal.Fifa estimated some $41bn would be added to the global economy, of which $17bn would boost the US economy alone, with 185,000 jobs created, mostly in hospitality and accommodation. But Alexander Budzier, a fellow in management practice at Oxford University and chief executive of project management company Oxford Global Projects, says the long-term economic benefits of hosting such a big sporting event just do not materialise.Host cities actually typically see a big drop in visitors, he says, as many seek to avoid the tournament chaos. And while there may be a spike in hiring, he argues it is typically only for lower-paid jobs in hospitality. "It creates jobs, but it does not create wealth," he says.Official figures show that hiring in US pubs, bars and restaurants ramped up ahead of the tournament in May, but the boom was short-lived. The only "worthwhile" economic benefit, Budzier argues, is the regeneration projects that can be done, such as the redevelopment and housing built in Stratford in London following the 2012 Olympic Games.But due to much of this World Cup using existing stadia, hotels, training complexes and travel infrastructure, "there won't be any economic benefits from development".The 2026 World Cup is on track to be the biggest gambling event of all time, with an estimated $50bn placed in bets - around $500m wagered per match, according to financial services firm Macquarie, which has interests in the gambling industry.It says this is primarily down to the expansion in teams, meaning there will be more than 100 matches played, up from 64 in 2022.Flutter Entertainment, which owns Paddy Power, Betfair and Sky Bet, forecast the amount placed in bets would be double that of the previous tournament because of growth in the US and also Brazil.Chad Beynon, an analyst at Macquarie, said in-play betting had taken over from the more traditional pre-match punt."Now it's all about reacting to what you're seeing on the field, adjusting your views. Whereas before it was kind of sit, watch, wait - you had to place your bet before the match," he says.Sports betting in the US is still a relatively new industry. Until 2018, betting on sports was only legal in Nevada, the home of Las Vegas, but a Supreme Court ruling paved the way for many states to legalise it.However, there are still some states where it remains illegal, including California and Texas. In those areas there has been big engagement in prediction markets - a fast-growing, billion-dollar industry popular with young men - which are not classified as gambling, meaning they can be used to place bets on sport regardless of which state someone is in.
contentParagraphs.zh: 虽然转播商不得不花一大笔钱来转播比赛，但收视率以及希望在电视上展示自己品牌的赞助商意味着他们也有可能在销售广告时段中大赚一笔。国际足联在本届世界杯上引入了备受关注的补水休息的内容，因凡蒂诺表示此举“纯粹是体育问题”，不会为管理机构带来额外收入。然而，球员在船上补水的三分钟时间为转播商和电视台提供了新的商业机会。据报道，福克斯体育公司斥资 4.85 亿美元购买了美国转播权，该公司将这些广告宣传为“由某个品牌赞助”。据专家称，福克斯体育频道每段 30 秒的世界杯广告时段平均花费在 20 万至 30 万美元之间。在美国比赛的最后阶段，这一数字高达 75 万美元。德意志银行研究部的拉布尔表示：“补水休息是纯粹的广告库存。如果它们消失，我会感到非常惊讶。扩展的格式将保留，因为规模现在是国际足联的商业模式。”在英国观看 BBC 或 ITV 比赛的球迷不会看到补水休息广告，因为前者不使用任何广告，而后者则受到监管机构关于广告金额的规定的限制。 60 分钟内使用的广告。世界杯的官方赞助商支付了令人瞠目结舌的巨额资金来将品牌与比赛联系起来，但毫无疑问最终获得了经济利益，阿迪达斯和可口可乐等品牌随处可见。这家德国运动服装品牌与其主要竞争对手耐克陷入了一场战斗，斥资约 5000 万英镑制作了由拉明·亚马尔 (Lamine Yamal)、裘德·贝灵厄姆 (Jude Bellingham) 和莱昂内尔·梅西 (Lionel Messi) 主演的“后院传奇”广告。不过，一些非官方品牌也受益于国际足联试图减少球迷看到它们的次数，例如旧金山李维斯体育场外的李维斯 (Levi's) 标志被遮盖起来。 美国、加拿大和墨西哥的 16 个主办城市一直在欢迎大量涌入的人潮粉丝和游客的数量促进了酒店业、酒店和当地企业的发展。尽管苏格兰人喝干了波士顿并赢得了这座城市及其人民的心，但专家表示，长期经济效益微乎其微。 国际足联估计，这将为全球经济增加约 410 亿美元，其中 170 亿美元将提振美国经济，创造 185,000 个就业岗位，其中大部分是酒店业和住宿业。但牛津大学管理实践研究员、项目管理公司 Oxford Global Projects 首席执行官亚历山大·巴齐尔 (Alexander Budzier) 表示，举办如此大型体育赛事的长期经济效益根本无法实现。 他表示，主办城市实际上通常会看到游客大幅下降，因为许多城市都试图避免赛事混乱。尽管招聘人数可能会激增，但他认为这通常只适用于酒店业的低薪工作。他说：“它创造了就业机会，但并没有创造财富。”官方数据显示，美国酒馆、酒吧和餐馆的招聘在 5 月份的比赛之前有所增加，但这种繁荣是短暂的。巴齐尔认为，唯一“有价值”的经济效益是可以进行的重建项目，例如 2012 年奥运会后在伦敦斯特拉特福进行的重建和住房建设。但由于本届世界杯的大部分内容都使用现有的体育场馆、酒店、训练场和旅游基础设施，“开发不会带来任何经济效益”。2026 年世界杯有望成为有史以来最大的赌博赛事，预计投入 500 亿美元赌注——根据对赌博业感兴趣的金融服务公司麦格理的数据，每场比赛的赌注约为 5 亿美元。该公司表示，这主要是由于球队的扩张，这意味着比赛数量将从 2022 年的 64 场增加到 100 多场。拥有 Paddy Power、Betfair 和 Sky Bet 的 Flutter Entertainment 预计，由于美国和巴西的增长，赌注金额将是上届锦标赛的两倍。麦格理分析师贝农表示，赛中投注已经取代了更传统的赛前投注。“现在，一切都是针对你在球场上看到的情况做出反应，调整你的观点。而以前是坐着、观看、等待——你必须在比赛前下注，”他说。在美国，体育博彩仍然是一个相对较新的行业。直到2018年，体育博彩仅在内华达州（拉斯维加斯的所在地）合法，但最高法院的一项裁决为许多州将其合法化铺平了道路。不过，仍有一些州仍然非法，包括加利福尼亚州和德克萨斯州。在这些地区，人们对预测市场的参与度很高，这是一个快速增长、价值数十亿美元的行业，深受年轻人欢迎，但预测市场不属于赌博，这意味着无论某人处于哪个州，预测市场都可以用来对体育赛事进行投注。

articles.title: A bug commonly sold at pet stores is helping scientists with a vexing task
articles.summary: The larvae of a beetle native to South and Central America, the critters are the perfect solution to sticky problem: How to prepare an animal skeleton for scientific use.
articles.titleZh: 宠物店常见的一种昆虫正在帮助科学家完成一项令人烦恼的任务
articles.summaryZh: 这些生物是原产于南美洲和中美洲的甲虫幼虫，是解决棘手问题的完美解决方案：如何准备用于科学用途的动物骨骼。

contentParagraphs.en: Superworms, the larvae of Zophobas morio, are often used as animal feed. But researchers have discovered they also like to feed on animal corpses and can be used to clean specimens.
contentParagraphs.zh: 超级蠕虫是 Zophobas morio 的幼虫，经常被用作动物饲料。但研究人员发现它们也喜欢以动物尸体为食，并且可以用来清洁标本。

contentParagraphs.en: MirekKijewski/iStockphoto/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: MirekKijewski/iStockphoto/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: MirekKijewski/iStockphoto/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: MirekKijewski/iStockphoto/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: For new discoveries, everyday mysteries, and the science behind the headlines, follow NPR's Short Wave podcast.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如需了解新发现、日常奥秘以及头条新闻背后的科学，请关注 NPR 的短波播客。

contentParagraphs.en: In the city of Mashhad in northeast Iran, there's a natural history museum associated with local Ferdowsi University that gets all manner of donations, including animals that died in the road and injured birds.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在伊朗东北部的马什哈德市，有一个与当地菲尔多西大学相关的自然历史博物馆，该博物馆收到了各种捐赠，包括路上死亡的动物和受伤的鸟类。

contentParagraphs.en: With all these specimens pouring in, the research team quickly ran out of space.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着所有这些标本涌入，研究小组很快就耗尽了空间。

contentParagraphs.en: "We don't have enough freezers to put these dead animals," says Niloofar Alaei Kakhki, a bioinformatician who's studied and worked there. "We have to find a way to clean them," she adds, so that these animals' skeletons can be studied and exhibited.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们没有足够的冰箱来存放这些死去的动物，”在那里学习和工作的生物信息学家 Niloofar Alaei Kakhki 说。 “我们必须找到一种方法来清洁它们，”她补充道，以便可以研究和展示这些动物的骨骼。

contentParagraphs.en: Researchers use a range of techniques to strip the flesh off an organism, but each has its drawbacks. In a paper published this month in PLOS One, Alaei and her colleagues propose an alternative — employing a suite of superworms, a kind of hefty beetle larva that can capably pick a variety of animal skeletons clean.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员使用一系列技术来剥离生物体的肉，但每种技术都有其缺点。在本月发表在《PLOS One》上的一篇论文中，Alaei 和她的同事提出了一种替代方案——使用一套超级蠕虫，这是一种巨大的甲虫幼虫，能够将各种动物的骨骼清除干净。

contentParagraphs.en: The worms work fast, yet they're "super gentle," says Alaei.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿莱伊说，这些蠕虫的工作速度很快，但它们“超级温柔”。

contentParagraphs.en: Introducing the superworm
contentParagraphs.zh: 超级蠕虫介绍

contentParagraphs.en: Among the existing methods for cleaning flesh off a specimen is chemical treatment, but that tends to be bad for the environment and can degrade the skeleton. Another option is boiling, but it's time-consuming and can damage "tiny, delicate bones," says Alaei.
contentParagraphs.zh: 现有的清除标本上肉的方法之一是化学处理，但这往往对环境不利，并且会降解骨骼。另一种选择是煮沸，但它很耗时，而且会损坏“微小而脆弱的骨头”，阿莱伊说。

contentParagraphs.en: A collection of superworms, each one nearly the size of a human finger, stripped this skeleton of a Eurasian eagle-owl of its flesh.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一群超级蠕虫，每条都接近人类手指大小，将这具欧亚雕鸮骨架上的肉剥掉了。

contentParagraphs.en: Morteza Monfared
contentParagraphs.zh: 莫尔泰萨·蒙法里德

contentParagraphs.en: Morteza Monfared
contentParagraphs.zh: 莫尔泰萨·蒙法里德

contentParagraphs.en: Then there's the use of another kind of insect, dermestid beetles, which quickly chew away the flesh, but if they escape, "it could be really dangerous for the museum collection," she says. That's because they're likely to destroy preserved specimens by chewing through feathers and dried skin.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后是使用另一种昆虫，皮甲虫，它们会迅速咀嚼肉，但如果它们逃脱，“这对博物馆藏品来说可能真的很危险，”她说。这是因为它们可能会通过咀嚼羽毛和干燥的皮肤来破坏保存下来的标本。

contentParagraphs.en: Then, some years ago, Alaei's colleagues in Iran turned their attention to Zophobas morio — the superworm, which is native to South and Central America. Each one is nearly the size of a human finger, they have large chewing mandibles, and they're available as feed at many pet stores. Superworms are usually devoured by other animals, but Alaei says she and her colleagues wondered: What if they did the eating instead, chewing up flesh to clean valuable specimens?
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后，几年前，Alaei 在伊朗的同事将注意力转向了 Zophobas morio——一种原产于南美洲和中美洲的超级蠕虫。每只都接近人类手指的大小，它们有大的咀嚼下颌，并且在许多宠物商店都可以作为饲料购买。超级蠕虫通常会被其他动物吃掉，但阿莱伊说，她和她的同事们想知道：如果它们改为吃肉，咀嚼肉来清洁有价值的标本，会怎么样？

contentParagraphs.en: Superworms transform into adult beetles only when they're isolated, explains Alaei. So as long as they're kept together, they're essentially locked in their larval state, unable to wreak havoc elsewhere in the museum.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿莱伊解释说，超级蠕虫只有在被隔离时才会转变为成年甲虫。因此，只要它们被关在一起，它们基本上就被锁定在幼虫状态，无法对博物馆的其他地方造成严重破坏。

contentParagraphs.en: "You can reuse the same larvae for almost six months," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “你可以重复使用相同的幼虫近六个月，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Superb skeletal sweepers
contentParagraphs.zh: 出色的骷髅清扫者

contentParagraphs.en: Alaei and her colleagues exposed superworms to dead animals of all sizes, ranging from mice, fish and small birds to wolves and wild cats. They pre-cleaned the specimens slightly, softened them in hot water for a few minutes, and then let the superworms have at them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿莱伊和她的同事将超级蠕虫暴露于各种大小的动物尸体中，从老鼠、鱼、小鸟到狼和野猫。他们预先稍微清洗了标本，在热水中软化了几分钟，然后让超级蠕虫吃掉它们。

contentParagraphs.en: Alaei says they did a remarkable job with no apparent side effects, even cleaning "the ribs of the fish, which is super, super tiny."
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿莱伊说，他们做得非常出色，没有明显的副作用，甚至清理了“超级非常小的鱼肋骨”。

contentParagraphs.en: To clean a specimen, the researchers found that 10 to 15 larvae work best. It's the right number to move things along at a reasonable clip without damaging the fragile bones.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员发现，为了清洁标本，10 到 15 只幼虫效果最好。这是以合理的速度移动物体而不损坏脆弱骨骼的正确数字。

contentParagraphs.en: Alaei, who's now based at the State Museum of Natural History in Stuttgart, Germany, hopes the superworm's success with cleaning skeletons, combined with their commercial availability, could be good news for all kinds of institutions, even small ones with less advanced facilities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿莱伊现供职于德国斯图加特国家自然历史博物馆，他希望超级蠕虫在清洁骨骼方面取得的成功，再加上其商业可用性，对各种机构来说都是好消息，即使是设施不太先进的小型机构。

contentParagraphs.en: "This study is adding one more possibility when you need to clean something," says Damien Charabidze, a forensic entomologist at the University of Lille in France who wasn't involved in the research.
contentParagraphs.zh: “当你需要清洁某些东西时，这项研究增加了一种可能性，”法国里尔大学法医昆虫学家达米安·查拉比泽（Damien Charabidze）说，他没有参与这项研究。

contentParagraphs.en: He says that superworms may well be easier to control than dermestid beetles, but he worries that the superworms' powerful mandibles could inadvertently snap a small bone. Plus, although they're omnivores, they prefer a vegetarian diet. So cadavers, he explains, are "not their usual food," and that could make them more finicky feeders.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说，超级蠕虫很可能比皮甲虫更容易控制，但他担心超级蠕虫强大的下颌骨可能会无意中折断一根小骨头。另外，虽然它们是杂食动物，但它们更喜欢素食。他解释说，因此尸体“不是它们通常的食物”，这可能会让它们对进食更加挑剔。

contentParagraphs.en: Marna Sakalem, an anatomist at the State University of Londrina in Brazil who didn't contribute to the research, raised a similar concern. And she adds that in the five years that she's worked with dermestid beetles, she and her team have not observed any escapees — so she's not willing to write off more traditional approaches just yet.
contentParagraphs.zh: 巴西隆德里纳州立大学的解剖学家玛娜·萨卡莱姆（Marna Sakalem）没有参与这项研究，她也提出了类似的担忧。她补充说，在她研究皮蠹的五年里，她和她的团队没有观察到任何逃逸者——所以她还不愿意放弃更传统的方法。

articles.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
articles.summary: Iran has retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting that a power and water plant had been hit.
articles.titleZh: 美国连续第七晚对伊朗发动空袭
articles.summaryZh: 伊朗对该地区的美国盟友进行了报复，科威特报告称一座发电厂和水厂遭到袭击。

contentParagraphs.en: The US military said it carried out the seventh night of strikes on Iran since President Donald Trump declared the temporary ceasefire agreement was "over".US Central Command (Centcom) said its forces "hit surveillance sites, military logistics infrastructure, underground weapons storage, and maritime capabilities".Iran said it responded by targeting US allies in the region including Kuwait, which said another of its power and water plants had been hit following a similar attack the previous day."Another electricity and water distillation plant was targeted by a hostile attack that led to a fire erupting in one of the plant's components," with some power generation units deactivated, the ministry of electricity and water said.Jordan's military also said it had intercepted 10 Iranian missiles fired into its airspace overnight. No damage was reported. Bahrain also said its air defences had "thwarted" Iranian attacks.Centcom said it had ended its strikes at 21:30 ET (02:30 BST) after several hours. "US forces employed fighter aircraft, aerial drones, and warships in addition to other assets," it said in its statement.Explosions were heard in the central city of Yazd and at Qeshm island and the port of Bandar Abbas next to the strait, Iranian state media reported.The US and Iran agreed to stop fighting in June to enable talks to end the war.The ceasefire was largely observed, even though there were Iranian attacks on oil tankers to force them to comply with Tehran's demands that ships seek authorisation to cross the Strait of Hormuz. The Iranian attacks on shipping were followed by US strikes against Iran.However, the talks appeared to make no headway and Trump declared the ceasefire over last week.Since then, in addition to attacking Iranian sites, the US has also re-imposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports.Iran has declared the Strait closed to shipping and traffic has largely stopped.A fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas used to transit through the strait and the head of the International Energy Agency, Fatih Birol, has said he has concerns about global energy supplies.The US military has also denied a report in Iran's Fars news agency that two oil tankers "exploded and caught fire while passing through a mined route south of the Strait of Hormuz". Centcom said: "Like most IRGC claims, this is false."On Friday, Iran's armed forces claimed to have attacked multiple US military facilities across the Gulf region in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan and – for the first time – Syria, which the US denied.Sources have told the BBC's US partner CBS news that several American service members were injured during Iranian attacks on two Jordanian bases over the past week.The US has meanwhile denied Tehran's earlier claims that the US attacked civilian infrastructure in Iran, including bridges, a train station and an airport. BBC Verify and BBC Persian have verified footage of damage to Gariveh Bridge, after night videos showed a ball of flames on top of it. Daylight images showed a crumbled stretch of road with rubble around the broken bridge. Provincial authorities in the affected region, Hormozgan province, said seven people were killed in the attacks.A White House spokesperson told the BBC the US had "carried out strikes exclusively on military targets, including military logistics infrastructure".The US also said it had destroyed a control tower in the port of Chabahar, with Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth sharing an image of the tower collapsing as it was hit by a strike. Centcom said the tower was part of an IRGC maritime surveillance network.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国军方表示，自唐纳德·特朗普总统宣布临时停火协议“结束”以来，它对伊朗进行了第七晚的袭击。美国中央司令部表示，其部队“袭击了监视地点、军事后勤基础设施、地下武器储存和海上能力”。伊朗表示，其回应是针对包括科威特在内的美国在该地区的盟友，科威特表示，在前一天发生类似袭击后，其另一座发电厂和水厂也遭到了袭击。电力和水利部表示，“核电站的一个组件发生了喷发”，一些发电机组已关闭。约旦军方还表示，已拦截了隔夜发射到其领空的 10 枚伊朗导弹。没有损坏的报告。巴林还表示，其防空系统“挫败”了伊朗的袭击。中央司令部表示，几个小时后，它已于美国东部时间 21:30（英国夏令时 02:30）结束了袭击。声明中称，“除了其他资产外，美军还动用了战斗机、无人机和军舰。”伊朗官方媒体报道称，在中部城市亚兹德、格什姆岛以及海峡附近的阿巴斯港均发生爆炸。美国和伊朗同意于六月停止战斗，以便通过谈判结束战争。尽管伊朗袭击了油轮，迫使它们遵守德黑兰对船只的要求，但停火协议在很大程度上得到了遵守。寻求穿越霍尔木兹海峡的授权。伊朗袭击航运之后，美国又对伊朗发动打击。不过，谈判似乎没有取得任何进展，特朗普上周宣布停火。此后，除了攻击伊朗地点外，美国还重新对伊朗港口实施海上封锁。伊朗已宣布该海峡关闭，航运和交通已基本停止。全球五分之一的石油和液化天然气用于过境该海峡，国际能源署署长法提赫比罗尔表示，他对全球能源供应感到担忧。美国军方还否认了伊朗法尔斯通讯社的报道，称两艘油轮“在经过霍尔木兹海峡以南的一条布有布雷的路线时爆炸并起火”。中央司令部表示：“与大多数伊斯兰革命卫队的说法一样，这是错误的。”周五，伊朗武装部队声称袭击了海湾地区的多个美国军事设施，包括科威特、巴林、约旦，以及首次袭击了叙利亚，但美国否认了这一说法。消息人士告诉英国广播公司美国合作伙伴哥伦比亚广播公司新闻，过去一周伊朗袭击约旦两个基地时，有几名美国军人受伤。与此同时，美国否认了德黑兰早些时候声称美国袭击了伊朗民用基础设施，包括桥梁、火车站和机场。 BBC verify 和 BBC Persian 已经核实了加里维桥受损的镜头，夜间视频显示桥顶有一团火球。日光图像显示断桥周围有一段破碎的道路，断桥周围有瓦砾。霍尔木兹甘省受影响地区的省级当局表示，袭击造成七人死亡。白宫发言人告诉英国广播公司，美国“专门针对军事目标进行了打击，包括军事后勤基础设施”。美国还表示，摧毁了恰巴哈尔港的一座控制塔，国防部长皮特·赫格斯分享了该塔在袭击中倒塌的照片。中央司令部表示，该塔是伊斯兰革命卫队海上监视网络的一部分。

articles.title: Federal Reserve looks for 'secret sauce' behind a successful Wisconsin toolmaker
articles.summary: Business is booming for Snap-on, a Wisconsin company that has made tools for professional mechanics for more than a century. It recently got a fact-finding visit from the head of the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank.
articles.titleZh: 美联储寻找威斯康星州成功工具制造商背后的“秘密武器”
articles.summaryZh: 实耐宝 (Snap-on) 是一家位于威斯康星州的公司，一个多世纪以来一直为专业机械师制造工具，该公司的业务正在蓬勃发展。 最近，芝加哥联邦储备银行行长对其进行了实地考察。

contentParagraphs.en: Federal Reserve looks for 'secret sauce' behind a successful Wisconsin tool maker
contentParagraphs.zh: 美联储寻找威斯康星州成功工具制造商背后的“秘密武器”

contentParagraphs.en: <iframe src="https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5894730/nx-s1-9852308" width="100%" height="290" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" title="NPR embedded audio player">
contentParagraphs.zh: <iframe src =“https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5894730/nx-s1-9852308”width =“100％”height =“290”frameborder =“0”scrolling =“no”title =“NPR嵌入式音频播放器”>

contentParagraphs.en: Snap-on sells custom tools to mechanics with a fleet of 3,400 rolling showrooms like this one. Its sales force visits nearly a million mechanics every week.
contentParagraphs.zh: Snap-on 向机械师销售定制工具，拥有 3,400 个像这样的滚动陈列室。其销售人员每周拜访近百万机械师。

contentParagraphs.en: Sign up for the Planet Money newsletter. The world is confusing. Economics can help.
contentParagraphs.zh: 订阅 Planet Money 时事通讯。世界很混乱。经济学可以提供帮助。

contentParagraphs.en: KENOSHA, Wis. — People are holding on to their vehicles longer these days, so spending on car repairs is on the rise.
contentParagraphs.zh: 威斯康星州基诺沙——如今，人们对车辆的保有时间越来越长，因此汽车维修支出也在增加。

contentParagraphs.en: That's good news for Snap-on. The company has been making high-end tools for auto mechanics for over a century.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这对于 Snap-on 来说是个好消息。一个多世纪以来，该公司一直致力于为汽车机械师制造高端工具。

contentParagraphs.en: "Vehicle repair is one of the great businesses," says CEO Nick Pinchuk. "Everybody's got to get their vehicles repaired. The garages are humming."
contentParagraphs.zh: “车辆维修是一项伟大的业务，”首席执行官尼克·平丘克 (Nick Pinchuk) 说道。 “每个人都必须修理他们的车辆。车库里嗡嗡作响。”

contentParagraphs.en: By catering to those mechanics, Snap-on has enjoyed steady growth and profitability, despite the challenges facing U.S. manufacturers. This week, the president of the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank, Austan Goolsbee, dropped by Snap-on headquarters in Kenosha, Wis., to get a taste of the company's secret sauce.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管美国制造商面临着挑战，但通过迎合这些机械师的需求，实耐宝仍实现了稳定的增长和盈利。本周，芝加哥联邦储备银行行长奥斯坦·古尔斯比 (Austan Goolsbee) 造访了位于威斯康星州基诺沙的 Snap-on 总部，品尝了该公司的秘密武器。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's interesting to see," Goolsbee says. "Customization is why they get paid a premium and how they still manufacture in America."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这很有趣，”古尔斯比说。 “定制是他们获得高薪的原因，也是他们仍然在美国制造的原因。”

contentParagraphs.en: The company makes 85,000 different tools, each one tailored to a specific need of someone fixing cars or airplanes or even rocket ships.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该公司生产 85,000 种不同的工具，每一种都是根据修理汽车、飞机甚至火箭飞船的特定需求量身定制的。

contentParagraphs.en: "Our philosophy is to be at the point of work, observing it, and figuring out what are the most sticky tasks," Pinchuk says. "And then using those insights to create a tool which will make it easier. People will pay for this."
contentParagraphs.zh: 平丘克说：“我们的理念是关注工作，观察工作，找出最棘手的任务。” “然后利用这些见解来创建一个工具，让事情变得更容易。人们会为此付出代价。”

contentParagraphs.en: Snap-on tools are not cheap. But they pay off if they help mechanics save time and complete more repairs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 实耐宝工具并不便宜。但如果它们能帮助机械师节省时间并完成更多维修，就会得到回报。

contentParagraphs.en: Snap-on franchisees make weekly visits to neighborhood mechanics in custom company vans. By closely watching mechanics at work, they can identify new tools that might be needed. They also provide credit for mechanics to purchase the tools.
contentParagraphs.zh: Snap-on 特许经营商每周都会乘坐公司定制货车拜访附近的机械师。通过密切观察工作中的机械师，他们可以识别可能需要的新工具。他们还为机械师购买工具提供信贷。

contentParagraphs.en: To diagnose those sticky tasks and peddle its tools, Snap-on relies on thousands of franchisees, who make weekly visits to nearly a million neighborhood mechanics in custom company vans.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了诊断这些棘手的任务并兜售其工具，Snap-on 依赖于数千名特许经营商，他们每周都会开着定制公司的货车拜访近百万名社区机械师。

contentParagraphs.en: Snap-on makes 80% of the tools it sells in the U.S. domestically, so it's relatively insulated from tariffs. To crank out such a wide variety of tools, its 15 U.S. factories have to be enormously flexible, changing models multiple times each day. Other companies do a much bigger volume of business with a much narrower product line. But Pinchuk says specialization is what makes Snap-on so profitable.
contentParagraphs.zh: 实耐宝在美国销售的工具中有 80% 是在美国国内生产的，因此相对而言不受关税影响。为了生产如此广泛的工具，其 15 家美国工厂必须非常灵活，每天都会多次更换型号。其他公司的业务量要大得多，但产品线要窄得多。但平丘克表示，专业化是 Snap-on 如此盈利的原因。

contentParagraphs.en: The company has deliberately avoided marketing its tools to the general public.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该公司刻意避免向公众推销其工具。

contentParagraphs.en: "If there's a rule in Snap-on that's irrevocable, it's that Thou Shalt Not Sell to Do-It-Yourself people," Pinchuk says. "Because it undermines the cachet of the brand. People use Snap-on tools to declare to the world they're doing something special."
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果 Snap-on 有一条不可撤销的规则，那就是你不得将产品卖给自己动手的人，”平丘克说。 “因为它损害了品牌的声誉。人们使用 Snap-on 工具向世界宣告他们正在做一些特别的事情。”

contentParagraphs.en: Snap-on employees Dave Wachsmuth (from left) and Matt Buckley explain the socket-making process to Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee. Snap-on makes 85,000 different tools, including 74 varieties of 10 mm sockets.
contentParagraphs.zh: Snap-on 员工 Dave Wachsmuth（左起）和 Matt Buckley 向芝加哥联邦储备银行行长 Austan Goolsbee 解释插座制作过程。实耐宝生产 85,000 种不同的工具，包括 74 种 10 毫米套筒。

contentParagraphs.en: That approach has instilled cradle-to-grave brand loyalty in generations of mechanics. They're often introduced to Snap-on tools while in technical school or during apprenticeships. Some even have their ashes stored in miniature Snap-on toolboxes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这种方法为一代又一代的机械师灌输了从摇篮到坟墓的品牌忠诚度。他们经常在技术学校或学徒期间接触到 Snap-on 工具。有些人甚至将骨灰存放在微型 Snap-on 工具箱中。

contentParagraphs.en: For Goolsbee, whose Fed district in the Upper Midwest has the nation's highest concentration of manufacturing, the visit to Snap-on offered an encouraging lesson in how — even in an uncertain environment — domestic manufacturers can specialize and prosper.
contentParagraphs.zh: 古尔斯比的美联储选区位于中西部北部，是全国制造业最集中的地区，对于古尔斯比来说，对实耐宝的访问提供了令人鼓舞的教训，让我们知道即使在不确定的环境中，国内制造商也可以实现专业化和繁荣。

contentParagraphs.en: "They scratch a very, very specific itch," Goolsbee says. "It's fun to see. This is where productivity growth comes from."
contentParagraphs.zh: “它们会抓到非常非常特殊的痒处，”古尔斯比说。 “这很有趣。这就是生产率增长的来源。”

articles.title: UK heatwave approaches two-week mark
articles.summary: All UK nations have experienced recent heatwave conditions but in southern England, the heatwave has now continued for 13 days. Sarah Keith-Lucas looks at how unusual this spell of heat is, and when we might see it easing.
articles.titleZh: 英国热浪接近两周大关
articles.summaryZh: 英国所有国家最近都经历了热浪天气，但在英格兰南部，热浪现已持续 13 天。莎拉·基思·卢卡斯 (Sarah Keith-Lucas) 着眼于这段时间的炎热天气有多么不寻常，以及我们何时会看到它的缓解。

contentParagraphs.en: This year is already shaping up to be an extraordinary one for weather records in the UK, having broken those for monthly temperature in both May and June by more than two degrees.This is the third heatwave of 2026. At the same point last year, we had also just seen the third heatwave of 2025. However, last July, some places in the south saw four consecutive days above 30C. So far this July we have now reached 12 days above 30C and 13 days in heatwave criteria across parts of England and Wales.Heatwave conditions have also gripped parts of Scotland and Northern Ireland this week but the heat has been less intense and has not lasted as long.Comparisons are being made with the historic hot summer of 1976. Whilst that year still holds the record for the longest heatwave, this year has seen higher extreme temperatures, and more "very hot days". The dryness as well as the heat has been particularly notable. Some places in southern England such as Wisley in Surrey have seen no rainfall in 30 days. Most of Wales and Northern Ireland have also had rainfall deficits, although parts of western Scotland have been wetter than average. That said, nowhere in the UK is currently in drought unlike the widespread one which accompanied the heatwave in 1976.As well as the duration and frequency of the heatwaves of 2026 so far, it has been the intensity that has been remarkable. This year has seen more days - six - in excess of 35C, and 35C has been recorded in three consecutive months (May, June and July) for the first time.
